Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for a nonvirtual subsystem inside an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a nonvirtual subsystem that is inside an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block. The incorrect generated code contains incorrect logic involving the output function of the nonvirtual subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for atomic subsystem with no outports and with Minimize algebraic loop occurrences parameter set to on
Description: The sorted order may be incorrect for a model containing an atomic subsystem with no outports and with the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  parameter set to on . For example, the?atomic subsystem may occur before the blocks that drive it even though the atomic subsystem is a direct-feed-through block. The incorrect sorted order may cause the following: 1. If the Enable local block outputs  parameter is set to on , the simulation and code generation results may not match because the generated code may contain a local variable whose value is used but not assigned.  2.??If the Enable local block outputs  parameter is set to off , there may be a hidden wrong answer because both the simulation and code generation results are incorrect.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:??(1)??Use the function turnOffMinAlgLoopFlagForSubsysWithoutOutports in the attached zip file?to automatically set MinAlgLoopOccurrences flag to??off?for subsystems without outports.?(2) ?Manually set the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences parameter to off?by entering the following command:? ? ? ? set_param(block, 'MinAlgLoopOccurrences', 'off');??Please note this parameter's default setting?is off, and this parameter is not shown?on the subsystem block parameters dialog box. ?
Products: MATLAB Coder, Embedded Coder, Simulink Coder

Summary: Chained indexed assignment?into result of an object method that returns a handle can crash MATLAB.
Description: The problem arises when an object has a method that returns a handle and a chained indexed expression involving a call to that method is used to assign into the?handle returned by?the method.?  For example, create MyHandle.m : classdef MyHandle < handle????  properties????????  handle_property;?????end end  Create ?Base.m:?  classdef Base????  properties????????  prop;????? end  ??? methods????????? function obj = Base(val)?????????????o bj.prop = val;????????  end?  ??????? function result = returnProperty(obj)????????????? result = obj.prop;???????? end????? end? end?  Calling obj = Base(Base(MyHandle)); obj(1).returnProperty.returnProperty.handle_property = 3;  causes the crash.? The?crash can occur even if?the handle?returned by the object method?is not?a member of the class.
Workaround: Split the indexing into two steps?by first?creating a temporary object and then?indexing into the temporary object.obj = Base(Base(MyHandle));?temp = obj(1).returnProperty.returnProperty;?temp.handle_property = 3;?To verify that the assignment succeeded, run:?isequal(obj(1).returnProperty.returnProperty.handle_property, 3)

Summary: The MATLAB Compiler and Applications that depend on the MATLAB Runtime may be affected by changes introduced by?macOS?10.12?(Sierra).
Description: On macOS?10.12 (Sierra) the MATLAB runtime does not show?localized user interfaces and messages for?Chinese, Japanese, and Korean?locales. Because of this, UIs, prompts, warnings, and error messages appear in English. The default character encoding is also incorrect for some locales. Character data created or loaded into applications using the runtime and re-saved might be corrupted in the process. MATLAB files containing non-ASCII characters might not execute as expected.? Examples of affected characters include Asian characters, accented characters, mathematical symbols, trademark symbols, and monetary characters such as the Euro symbol. The following language and region combinations?are compatible with macOS 10.12 (Sierra) and no further action is required:  Deutsch, Austria Deutsch, Switzerland English, United States English, Australia English, Canada English, United Kingdom Fran?ais, Canada Fran?ais, Switzerland Espa?ol, Spain Nederlands, Belgium Portugu¨şs, Brazil Portugu¨şs, Portugal  (You can see what Language & Region your Mac is set to in the "Language & Region" Preferences Pane.) If your language and region combination is not listed above, please refer to the Workaround section.?
Workaround: For MATLAB R2013b and prior releases, please delay installation of macOS 10.12 (Sierra) and contact Technical Support.Installation Instructions for MATLAB R2014a through R2016bTo install this patch, you need the username and password of an account with Admin privileges.Download the attachment.Look for the file where your web browser saves downloaded files.If your web browser did not unzip the patch file for you, double-click the file in the Finder to unzip it.Double-click the Disk Image (.dmg file) to mount and open the disk image.At the top level of the mounted disk image, double-click R20XXx_patch_1468195.app to launch the installer.Locate and select your MATLAB Runtime installation (for example, v91), and click Choose. Click OK to confirm the release of MATLAB Runtime you are patching.Enter the name and password of an account with Admin privileges.When the upgrade is complete, you will see the message "Patch complete!"

Summary: Referenced model contains enabled subsystem inside a export-function model can cause incorrect simulation results in multirate multitasking harness model
Description: When all of the followings are true, Simulation produces incorrect results: A model has an?enabled subsystem.? There is a Model block of above model?in a function-call subsystem driven by an?function-call?port.?This is an export-function model at this level. The sample time of the function-call port is a valid positive value.  The export-function model is in a simulation harness model that has multirate rates and is in multitasking mode.  The function ports on the export-function model are?driven by?caller running at a subrate.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for S-function with non-scalar signal input can produce incorrect numerical results
Description: The generated code for the S-function block (built with system target file rtwsfcn.tlc ) can produce incorrect numerical results when the following conditions are true: The generated S-function block?has at least 1 input port.  One or more input ports to the S-function block has a non-scalar dimension.  Multiple upstream blocks contribute to the non-scalar signal line that connects to the input port or ports of the S-function block. For example, a Mux block creates the non-scalar signal line.   These conditions can cause the input signal data to the S-function block to reside in non-contiguous memory. Because the S-function requires that the input data is contiguous, the generated code can produce incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: Use either of these workaroundsIf using a previously generated S-function block, insert a Signal Conversion block immediately before each non-scalar input to the S-function. This Signal Conversion block directs the code generator to make a copy of the signal in a contiguous memory region that the S-function reads. Configure the Signal Conversion block as follows:Set Output: to Signal CopyEnable Exclude this block from 'Block Reduction' optimizationIf generating the S-function block (with the S-function system target file rtwsfcn.tlc), before generating code, configure either of these Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters options for the block as follows. Either of these options cause the generated code for the S-function not to?expect a contiguous memory region for non-scalar inputs:Disable Use memcpy for vector assignmentSet the Memcpy threshold value to a large number (for example, intmax: 2147483647)

Summary: Global Data Store block that accessed in Public Simulink function that called in Initialize/Reset/Terminate functions and other function may cause Simulink crash.
Description: 1. A global DSM is accessed from?more than 2?functions (Simulink Function, Export FcnCalls, or IRT functions), AND2. One of these fucntions is?public?Simulink function. AND 3. This public Simulink Function is?called?in more than 1 other functions (Simulink function, Export Function Call system, IRT functions).?AND 4. The number of function accesses the global DSM block is?more?than number functions call public Simulink functions.  ?When all of above conditions are true, Simulink may crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect variable assignment to 0.0 or -0.0 in the true and false branches of if statement
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect C code for a Simulink model that contains the following MATLAB code:  if p?  ?? y=0;?  else?  ?? y=-0;?  end  The C code may be optimized such  that y  is set equal to 0.0  or -0.0  regardless of whether the If  statement evaluates to true or false. Note that the above MATLAB code may be explicit in a Simulink model (in a MATLAB function block, for example). The above MATLAB code may also occur during the code generation process and thus be in the model as a combination of blocks that produce the same logic. The attached model contains an example of when this bug occurs in a MATLAB Function block.? ?

Summary: Models containing matrices larger than 268,435,455 bytes may not generate code or simulate in accelerated mode
Description: Models containing matrices or vectors larger than 268,435,455 bytes may fail to generate code with the following error message: CGIR assertion 'isLessThanZero(aSize)' failed in...'  This error may also?occur for?Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator, Software in the Loop, or Processor in the Loop simulation modes. The matrix or vector must be larger than 268,435,455 (2^28-1) bytes. ?This amount is equivalent to ?33,554,432 elements of type double , or 67,108,864 elements of type single  / int32 . ?

Summary: Inconsistency between ModelAdvisor.run API command line output and actual Model Advisor report
Description: The command line summary from ModelAdvisor.run()  API can be inconsistent with the actual Model Advisor reports if the previous Model Advisor run took place in the same working directory?that was not cleared.
Workaround: Clear working directory slprj folder before using ModelAdvisor.run API.

Summary: Protected model creation fails to locate configuration sets located in data dictionaries when the original model is using configset reference
Description: When trying to create a protected model while using a data dictionary, Simulink produces the following error message: Could not evaluate 'CodeGenerationConfigurationSet' in data dictionary Caused by: Error occurred.

Summary: Crash when running a model after expanding a subsystem whose output was marked for streaming
Description: If you expand a Simulink subsystem with any of its outputs marked for streaming, subsequently running the model results in a crash. The generated stack trace begins with:? [ ?0] 0x0000000027b915f0 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2016a\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+02430448 InlineVariantBlock::IsBlockInlineVariantBlock+00000000 [ ?1] 0x00000001050dbfdf C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2016a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+17481695 sl_hmi::StreamingClients::getCount+00001071?
Workaround: To prevent the crash:Disable streaming for signals on the output port of the subsystem block you wish to expand.?Expand the subsystem.Mark signals for streaming.You can select signals for streaming using the streaming menu or the?Simulink.sdi.markSignalForStreaming?command.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Certain operations might be evaluated unexpectedly when used in a logical expression in a MATLAB Function block
Description: If you use certain operations like cast or array indexing as part of a logical expression in a MATLAB Function block, the operations might get evaluated outside of the logical expression in the generated code, potentially leading to a crash.? For example, an if statement such as: if u <= arrayLen && int32(myArray(u)) < 5  might generate the following code: tmp = myArray[u-1];? if (tmp < MAX_INT) {? ? ?if (tmp > MIN_INT) {? ? ? ? tmp_0 = tmp;? ? ?} else {? ? ? ? tmp_0 = MIN_INT;? ? ?}? } else {?? ? ?tmp_0 = MAX_INT;? }? if (u <= arrayLen && tmp_0 < 5) {?...? ? Notice that the array is indexed in the first line, before checking whether or not the index is valid.
Workaround: Break up the array indexing or cast operation into a separate assignment and use a second if statement. For example:if u <= arrayLen ? ? temp = int32(myArray(u));? ? if temp < 5? ? ...?

Summary: On Windows, remote desktop interactions?during long simulations can cause Simulink to stop responding
Description: On Windows, if you are running a very long simulation, starting or stopping a remote desktop?session can cause Simulink?to stop responding. This issue can occur when you: Start a simulation on your desktop and then start a?remote desktop session to that desktop Start a remote?desktop session?to your?desktop, start the simulation, and end the remote desktop session
Workaround: To make Simulink respond when it is?in this state, pause and continue the simulation from the command line:set_param('Model','SimulationCommand','Pause');set_param('Model','SimulationCommand','Continue');?

Summary: The sdruload command can error out with?"No applicable UHD devices found" for USRP X300 or X310 radio
Description: The?sdruload? command can error out with?"No applicable UHD devices found" for USRP X300 or X310 radio. You might not be able to update the FPGA and firmware image on the radio.
Workaround: To?avoid the error, wait for a few seconds before replying yes to this question:?Would you like to continue? [yes/no]

Summary: Stateflow transitions are drawn on the wrong subviewer
Description: When you create a library containing a hierarchy of subcharted boxes (of depth greater than three levels) and supertransitions, it is possible?that when you save and reload the model, the transitions in the deepest part of the hierarchy are drawn on the wrong subviewer.

Summary: In MATLAB Function Block, using concatenation to initialize a dynamic matrix generates incorrect answers
Description: In MATLAB Function Block or MATLAB script, when you initialize a dynamic matrix, such as: y1 = [y1; [1 2 3 4] ];  the code generator generates incorrect dimensions that result in wrong answers. The concatenation used for initializing values into y1? generates incorrect dimension information. It also initializes y1  with incorrect values.  This bug?might occur when you write a horizontal concatenation expression that causes read-write issue of initializing memory for a dynamically allocated matrix.
Workaround: Quick workaround is to change the order of variables in the concatenation expression, such as:y1 = [ [1,2,3,4]; y1]

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder can produce unexpected code interface for a Simulink Function block connected to a root Outport block
Description: When you have a Simulink Function block connected to a root Outport block, unexpected code can be produced if all of the following conditions are met:  You are using Export-Function modeling style The signal dimensions of the root Outport block is greater than one The signals in the connection to the root Outport block cannot be uniquely identified with a single block (for example, if you use a Selector block or Demux block to perform partial selection of the signal; or, if you use Mux blocks and Demux blocks to rearrange the elements in the signal)
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block immediately after any Mux, Demux, or Selector blocks on the signal path. ?By doing this, you are modifying your model so that the signal driving the root Outport block is uniquely identified with the Signal Conversion block. Select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization check box in the Signal Conversion block parameter dialog box.You can also automate the workaround process by using the script, Detect_1453109, provided in the attached ZIP file. To use the script, call with the model of interest, at the MATLAB command prompt: ?>> result = Detect_1453109('myModelName') ?If the model did not have any occurrence of the bug, then result will be 0. If the model does have the occurrence of the bug, then result will be 1, and a link to fix the bug will also be provided. Click on the fix link to open, and update the model to automatically apply the Signal Conversion block workaround described above.

Summary: Error upon following the example "Model RF Filter Using Equivalent Baseband" using SimRF in R2015b and R2016a.
Description: Upon following the steps mentioned to create the demo - "Model RF Filter Using Equivalent Baseband" in the following link:  http:/?/?www.mathworks.com/ ?help/?simrf/?gs/?model-an-lc-bandpass-filter.html  You get the following error in R2015b and R2016a:  Error occurred in 'example_test/?Output Port'. Error in 'example_test/?Output Port/?Check Input Signal/?Check Signal Attributes1/?Check Signal Attributes': Input signal frame status and dimensions match those specified on the block dialog box. Component: Simulink | Category: Block error  Error occurred in 'example_test/?Output Port'. Error in port widths or dimensions. Output port 1 of 'example_test/?Output Port/?Check Input Signal/?Check Signal Attributes1/?In1' is a [128x1] matrix. ?The Equivalent Baseband Input block expects a frame type input when the "Input Processing" parameter is set to "Columns as channels (frame based)". However, as of R2015b, the  output of the Sine Wave block is identified only as a regular column vector not a frame.?
Workaround: In R2015b and R2016a,? the user needs to add a Frame Conversion block (from DSP System Toolbox > Signal Management > Signal Attributes) after the Matrix Sum block before running the example. This Frame conversion block explicitly converts a column vector to a frame.As of R2016b,? the Sine Wave block creates a frame  based output and the user does not need to add a ?Frame Conversion block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect representation of down-counting array subscript with constant unsigned start and stop value
Description: MATLAB Coder might incorrectly represent a colon expression used to subscript an array when?the colon expression?satisfies all of the following conditions: The starting, increment, and ending values are compile-time constants The starting and ending values have an unsigned integer type The increment value is negative  For example, consider the?MATLAB function: function issue_1452753()A=[17    24     1     8    15;   23     5     7    14    16;    4     6    13    20    22;   10    12    19    21     3;   11    18    25     2     9]; temp = A(uint32(10):-1:uint32(2));showTemp(temp);end function showTemp(temp)coder.inline('never');disp(temp);end Executing?issue_1452753 ?in MATLAB produces the output:? 18 ? ?12 ? ? 6 ? ? 5 ? ?24 ? ?11 ? ?10 ? ? 4 ? ?23  If you generate a MEX file issue_1452753_mex?by using?MATLAB Coder and run it, you get an incorrect output:   18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18  See also Bug Report ID 1868474 .?
Workaround: Use a signed integer type for the starting and ending values of the colon expression.function issue_1452753()A=[17    24     1     8    15;       23     5     7    14    16;    4     6    13    20    22;   10    12    19    21     3;   11    18    25     2     9]; temp = A(int32(10):-1:int32(2));end function showTemp(temp)coder.inline('never');disp(temp);end

This behavior is more likely to happen when the fields have data types whose sizes are not multiples of eight (for example, boolean data type).  This bug occurs during simulation only, and the generated code is still correct.

Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR model with latched root inport feeding Switch block incorrectly generates multiple Rte_Read API calls
Description: AUTOSAR code generation incorrectly generates multiple Rte_Read  API calls if all  of the following conditions are true: In the model, a root inport directly feeds a subsystem. The root inport is configured for AUTOSAR explicit read access. In the subsystem, the inport block corresponding to the root inport is latched. This includes Simulink server function subsystems for which all inports are latched. The latched subsystem inport directly feeds a Switch block data input.  Under these conditions, the generated C code contains multiple Rte_Read  API calls. This is incorrect because explicit read access APIs should not be called more than once in an AUTOSAR runnable task.
Workaround: To work around this bug, change the AUTOSAR data access mode for the root inport from explicit read to implicit read.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect code generation for nested handle objects with different lifetimes
Description: Code generation may generate incorrect code for MATLAB code with all of these conditions: An outer object that contains another handle object as its property. The inner object is assigned inside the constructor of the outer object. The lifetime of the inner object is greater than the lifetime of the outer object (there is a function whose caller(s) can access the inner object but not the outer object).  For example, the following MATLAB program meets these conditions and generates incorrect code: function y = foo(n)    o = sub(n);        y = o.p;endfunction o = sub(n)    coder.inline('never')    obj = MyOuter(n);    o = obj.p;end? classdef MyOuter < handle    properties        p;    end    methods        function o = MyOuter(n)            o.p = MyInner(n);        end    end?? end  In this program, the inner object can be accessed in function foo  because it is returned from function sub , but the lifetime of the outer object is limited only to the function sub .

Summary: DocBlock content can appear as hyperlinks in Word reports
Description: For reports generated using Report Explorer, DocBlock RTF and HTML content can appear as hyperlinks in Word reports. The content appears as hyperlinks if you use the Word (from template)  output option to generate a report from a model containing RTF or HTML DocBlocks, close the generated report, and reopen the report in Microsoft Word.
Workaround: After generating a Word report containing DocBlock content, execute this command:docview(REPORT_PATH,'UpdateFields','unlinkdocxsubdoc');REPORT_PATH is the path of your report. This command replaces the links to the DocBlock content with the content.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes on exit when matlabroot/toolbox is on MATLAB search path
Description: If the folder matlabroot /toolbox  is added to the search path, MATLAB crashes on exit. A stack is displayed that includes the libicuuc  library and the libmwmpath  library. matlabroot   is the folder displayed when you type matlabroot  in the Command Window.
Workaround: Do not add the folder matlabroot/toolbox to the MATLAB search path. The toolbox folder itself does not ordinarily contain MATLAB executable files, and should not need to be on the search path. Subfolders of matlabroot/toolbox can safely be added to the path.To remove the matlabroot/toolbox folder from the search path, run this command:rmpath(fullfile(matlabroot, 'toolbox'))

Summary: Text component does not honor beginning or ending white space in template-based Word output
Description: The Report Explorer's Text component does not honor beginning or ending white space in template-based Word output. For example, suppose you create this setup: Paragraph +Text - See  +Link - "Results" The output appears as: See"Results"' ?
Workaround: Use a nonbreaking space at the beginning or end of a Text component, for example:Paragraph +Text - See%<char(160)> +Link - "Results"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results because of uninitialized local variables for Unit Delay blocks
Description: The generated code may contain uninitialized local variables leading to a mismatch between simulation and code generation results for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains one or multiple Switch blocks. The output of one Switch block connects to a Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block connects to another Switch block if there are multiple Switch blocks or the input port of the only Switch block if there is just one Switch block. If there are multiple Switch blocks, the second Switch block passes the input from the Unit Delay block when the value of its second input evaluates to true.
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file, workaroundForBugReport1437314.p. Insert the P-file on the MATLAB file path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command: run workaroundForBugReport1437314.p?

Summary: Incorrect AUTOSAR RTE function calls generated for root inport with explicit receive data access that branches to pass-through nonreusable subsystem
Description:
In AUTOSAR generated C code, RTE functions for explicit receive data access (for example, Rte_Read_

_ ) should not be called more than once in the same time step. The port element value is not latched, which means that multiple RTE receive calls in the same time step could yield different values. An AUTOSAR model build might generate multiple RTE receive calls in the same time step if all of these conditions exist: A root inport in the model is configured for one of the following AUTOSAR data access modes: ExplicitReceive , EndToEndRead , IsUpdated , or QueuedExplicitReceive . The root inport branches to at least two destinations. One of the root inport destinations is a nonreusable subsystem that passes data directly through, with no states between the nonreusable susbystem inport and outport. For example, the nonreusable subsystem inport connects to a Gain block, which connects to the nonreusable subsystem outport. Under these conditions, the generated model C code might call the RTE function for the branched root inport more than once in the same time step.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform these steps:Add a Signal Conversion block to the model.Select the Signal Conversion block option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.Connect the Signal Conversion block input to the root inport with the issue.Adding the Signal Conversion block forces the RTE function generated for the branched root inport to be called only once in the root runnable function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR model with implicit read inside Stateflow function generates incorrect RTE API name
Description: AUTOSAR code generation generates an incorrect RTE API name if both  of the following conditions are true: A root inport configured with AUTOSAR implicit read access directly feeds a Stateflow chart. In the chart, a Stateflow function accesses the root inport.  Under these conditions, the generated Rte_IRead  API name contains an incorrect runnable name.
Workaround: To work around this bug, add a Signal Conversion block between the root inport and the Stateflow chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for models involving messages may not be deployable to platforms that assign different memory sizes to the data carried by the messages
Description: The generated code for Simulink models involving messages has hard-coded memory sizes for the data carried by the messages. These memory sizes correspond to the data types of the message data, and are computed on the host platform. So the generated code might not be portable across platforms that use different memory sizes for those data types. For example, if you generate code for messages on a Windows platform, you might not be able to deploy it to a macOS platform.Below are examples of hard-coded sizes for message payload types:  Example 1:  msg = slMsgSvcCreateMsg(DW.slMsgMgr_Impl, (void*)&B.out, 8U, DW.qId); instead of:msg = slMsgSvcCreateMsg(DW.slMsgMgr_Impl, (void*)&B.out, sizeof(real_T), DW.qId); Example 2:  tmp = slMsgSvcGetMessageData(msg);memcpy(B.out, tmp, 8U); instead of:memcpy(B.out, tmp, sizeof(real_T)); Example 3:  slMsgSvcCreateFIFOMsgQueue(DW.slMsgMgr_Impl, DW.qId, 16, 16U, ...) instead of:slMsgSvcCreateFIFOMsgQueue(DW.slMsgMgr_Impl, DW.qId, 16, sizeof(real_T)*2, ...)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using an undefined variable as an index into a matrix or cell array might produce an incorrect answer
Description: When indexing into a matix or cell array with an expression that uses a variable that is not defined, the result in generated code might be different from the result in MATLAB? . Consider the following function: function res=foo(in1) if in1 > 0     x = 2; end if in1 == 0     x = 3; end a = 1:5; res = a(x);   If  in1  is less than zero, x  is not defined. MATLAB produces this error message:  Undefined function or variable 'x'.   When the code generation products, such as MATLAB?  Coder? and Simulink?  Coder?,  detect that an undefined variable is used in an expression that indexes into a matrix or cell array, they produce an error message like: ??? Variable 'x' is not fully defined on some execution paths  However, in some cases, the code generator fails to detect this situation. Instead,  it assigns the variable an arbitrary value in the generated code. This bug occurs only when the original MATLAB code fails to assign a variable before using it in an expression  that  indexes into a matrix or cell array. In these cases, the code fails in MATLAB.
Workaround: Define all variables used in an expression that indexes into a matrix or  cell array. For the previous example, define x on all execution paths that lead to res = a(x). function res=foo(in1) x = 1; % ADDED TO RESOLVE THE BUGif in1 > 0     x = 2; end if in1 == 0     x = 3; end a = 1:5; res = a(x);

Summary: DOM API does not honor?different odd and even page headers and footers setting in?a Word template
Description: The DOM API does not honor different first page header and footer and different odd and even page header and footer settings in a Word ?template. If the template contains any even or first page header or footer,  the output document uses different first page and even and odd page headers and footers,  regardless of the settings for these options in the template.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inport block inside a function-call subsystem does not latch feedback signal when connected to a?conditionally executed?input branch
Description: When an Inport block?inside a function-call subsystem selects the Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs  parameter, it should copy the value of the corresponding input to the subsystem before executing any other blocks inside this subsystem. This prevents the input value from changing during the execution of the subsystem. However, when the Inport bock is connected to the input of a block that supports conditional input branch execution, such as a Switch block, the Inport block fails to latch the subsystem input value. The example model mG1430178.slx in the attached ZIP-file shows the problem. When simulating the model or executing the generated code of the model, yout.signals(1).values is different from yout.signals(2).values. The logged values of Out2 are correct, while the values of Out1 are wrong.  The script checkForG1430178.m also in the attached ZIP-file detects the problem. In the MATLAB Command Window, enter ????? unlatchedInport = checkForG1430178('mG1430178')   The script lists the Inport blocks that fail to latch. If the return value for unlatchedInport is: Not empty, the model has latch problems. For the example model mG1430178, the return value is??? 1ˇÁ2 cell array??? 'mG1430178/Inport has latch problem/Function-Call Subsystem/feedback' [1]  Empty, the model is not affected by this problem.??? 0ˇÁ2 empty cell array
Workaround: Consider one of the following workarounds:Clear the Configuration Parameters> Optimization > Advanced parameters > Conditional input branch execution checkbox, or in the MATLAB Command Window, enter????? set_param(modelname, 'ConditionallyExecuteInputs', 'off')For the Inport block, clear the 'Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs' check box.Insert a Function-Call Feedback Latch block before the corresponding subsystem input port.

Summary: Skipping holes in table entries can corrupt a DOM-generated Word document
Description: Skipping a hole in a table entry can corrupt a Word document generated by the DOM. This corruption occurs if the skipped hole is the table entry's only content. For example, suppose the hole named MyData  is the only hole in a table entry in a Word template named MyTemplate.dotx . This script generates a Word document that you cannot open because it is corrupt. import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('myreport','docx','MyTemplate');append(rpt,'My Report');while ~strcmp(rpt.CurrentHoleId,'#end#')    switch rpt.CurrentHoleId       case 'MyData'       % skipping this hole    end    moveToNextHole(rpt);endclose(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath); % Causes Word to display an error message.
Workaround: Include a space or other fixed content in the table entry before or after the hole.

Summary: Using a MATLAB structure variable with one or more fields declared using coder.opaque may cause an error during code generation
Description: Generating code for a model that calls into MATLAB code which uses structure variables with one or more fields declared using coder.opaque  function may produce the following error during code generation: ?CGIR assertion 'isKnown()' failed in '../../derived/glnxa64/src/include/cg_ir/util_prim/../../../../../../src/cg_ir/export/include/cg_ir/util_prim/Size64.hpp:120'  ?
Workaround: Increasing the value of the Maximum stack size parameter may remove this error. The Maximum stack size parameter is on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters pane.

Summary: Accessing properties or methods of a malformed object can crash MATLAB
Description: When an object cannot be constructed because of an error, the result can be a malformed object. Attempting to call the methods or access the properties of a malformed object can crash MATLAB. A malformed object can arise from loading an object from a MAT-file, if an object or one of its properties cannot be constructed. It can also arise in the context of a parfor  loop if the workers need to access an object that cannot be constructed.

Summary: Dynamic DAQ/STIM list communication might fail in some measurement combinations
Description: Some combinations of measurements used in a dyanamic DAQ or STIM list using XCP can result in an error during runtime communication with an XCP slave module. In this situation, communication with the slave might fault with the following error message: Assertion failed ERROR: Illegal packetsize 11 for CANAssertion failed

Summary: MATLAB might crash or return unexpected results when a model contains a MATLAB Discrete-Event System block whose code does not implement the destroyImpl or exitImpl methods
Description: MATLAB might crash or return unexpected results, when a SimEvents model contains a MATLAB Discrete-Event System block whose System object code has one of these conditions: A destroy event for an entity is scheduled, but the System object does not define a destroyImpl  method for this entity type. A forward event for an entity is scheduled, but the System object does not define an exitImpl  method for this entity type.  In these cases, the System object fails to cancel the entity events scheduled to happen after destroy and exit, and still triggers the related event actions. ?
Workaround: Define empty destroyImpl and/or exitImpl methods for each associated entity type in your MATLAB Discrete-Event System block code.

Summary: Simulink Editor appears not to respond to input when Save As dialog box is obscured
Description: When you close a dirty model, the Save As dialog box can be placed behind other windows. The Simulink Editor does not accept input when the dialog box is open. Because you cannot see the dialog box, it appears that Simulink is not responding.  On Windows, this issue applies to R2016a. On Linux, it applies to R2015b and earlier.
Workaround: If it appears that the Simulink Editor is not responding, minimize that window to see if a Save As dialog box is open. Respond to the dialog box to continue editing.

Summary: Potentially incorrect reset logic in the generated HDL code for Delay blocks with reset
Description: When optimizations are requested on a subsystem that contains Delay blocks with reset ports,? the reset logic in the generated HDL code may be incorrect. The symptoms are that a switch intended to supply the correct value to the register has its inputs switched in the generated code.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, choose one of the following:Add an Enable port to the block, and drive it with a constant '1' or 'true' value. Use the Synchronous mode of the State Control block. This block is located in the HDL Coder > HDL Subsystems library. This block provides more efficient reset and enable semantics for the subsystem hierarchy in which it resides, and generates cleaner HDL code. For more information about the State Control block, see the HDL Coder documentation.

Summary: Block names are lost in Model Advisor generated Word or PDF format report
Description: When Model Advisor generates a report in Word or PDF format,?the path to Simulink blocks can be?lost. Instead of showing?the path of the violated blocks, Model Advisor generates?some internal flags.?

Summary: Code generation error for 2-D AUTOSAR.Parameter data object used in referenced model
Description: In an AUTOSAR model, if a referenced model uses an AUTOSAR.Parameter  data object configured for two dimensions, code generation might fail with an error similar to the following: Value for AUTOSAR.Parameter object "Table" is not scalar or one-dimensional. Specify a one-dimensional value for the objector select the option 'Support root-level matrix I/O using one-dimensional arrays' on Model Configuration Parameters >Code Generation > AUTOSAR Code Generation Options. The workaround given in the error message does not resolve the error.
Workaround: To work around this bug, replace the referenced model that uses the 2-D AUTOSAR.Parameter data object with an atomic subsystem.

Summary: Crash during simulation of model reference hierarchy linked to dictionary hierarchy
Description: When you simulate a model, MATLAB can crash if?all of these conditions are true: The model references other models (through Model blocks). The models use data dictionaries. For example,?a hierarchy of referenced dictionaries?matches the hierarchy of referenced models. In one of the referenced models, a signal line?acquires settings from a Simulink.Signal  object?(which resides in one of the dictionaries). The InitialValue  property of the object is set to a value other than the default.
Workaround: Use?one of these workarounds:Explicitly update the model diagram (for example, Ctrl+D)?before trying to simulate.Link all of the models to the same data dictionary (the top-level dictionary).

For example, this script produces a bad link: import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('badlink','pdf');target = 'https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:×î˝ü¤ÎłöŔ´ĘÂ';img = Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg'));area = ImageArea(target,'Wikipedia Site',0,0,100,100);map = ImageMap;append(map,area);img.Map = map;append(d,img);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect constant value when generating code for coding pattern involving multiple for loop style while loops that can be combined
Description: MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code with an incorrect constant for a coding pattern involving:? Multiple for  loop style while loops that execute the same number of iterations. A while loop induction variable is used outside of a loop  MATLAB Coder contains an optimization that tries to combine multiple loops with the same number of iterations into a single loop. When loops are combined, if any use of an induction variable exist after the combined loop, it might be replaced with a constant. ?This constant is intended to represent the value of the induction variable after the original loop, but is internally computed incorrectly. For an example see issue_1416439. Here is the MATLAB code in that example:? function [out] = issue_1416439()out = 0;inductionVar1 = 0;while inductionVar1 < 10    inductionVar1 = inductionVar1 + 1;endinductionVar2 = 0;while inductionVar2 < 10    out = out + inductionVar1;    inductionVar2 = inductionVar2 + 1;endout = out + inductionVar2;end The value of 'inductionVar1' after execution of the first for-loop style while loop is 10. In the generated code the two loops are combined and the use of 'inducationVar1' is replaced with the constant '9.0' which is incorrect:double issue_1416439(void){  double out;  int inductionVar1;  out = 0.0;  for (inductionVar1 = 0; inductionVar1 < 10; inductionVar1++) {    out += 9.0;  }  out += (double)inductionVar1;  return out;} The correct code can be seen below:double issue_1416439(void){  double out;  int inductionVar1;  out = 0.0;  for (inductionVar1 = 0; inductionVar1 < 10; inductionVar1++) {    out += 10.0;  }  out += (double)inductionVar1;  return out;}? This issue affects the following code generation workflows: Code generation using MATLAB Coder only Code generation from a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function block, MATLAB System block, or MATLAB Function in Stateflow

Summary: DOM API does not honor HAlign format for tables in a PDF report
Description: The DOM API does not honor HAlign format for tables in a PDF report. The format is applied to the table entries instead.For example, the PDF output from this code centers the table entries instead of the table. import mlreportgen.dom.*;d = Document('myreport','pdf');open(d);  data = { 'NAMES'; ...'John'; ...'Smith'; ...'Robert'; ...'Mark' };  t = Table(data); t.Border = 'solid';t.BorderWidth = '3px';t.RowSep = 'solid';t.RowSepWidth = '1px';t.HAlign = 'center';  % To highlight the first rowt.row(1).Style = {BackgroundColor('skyblue') Color('white') Bold()};  append(d,t);  close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);
Workaround: To center a table:Create an invisible, 3-column wrapper table.Append the table you want to center to the second column of the wrapper table.Set the width of the wrapper table's second column to a value large enough to accommodate the main table.This script shows the centering procedure:import mlreportgen.dom.*;d = Document('myreport','pdf');open(d);data = {'NAMES'; ...'John'; ...'Smith'; ...'Robert'; ...'Mark' };t = Table(data); t.Border = 'solid';t.BorderWidth = '3px';t.RowSep = 'solid';t.RowSepWidth = '1px';t2 = Table({[],t,[]});t2.Border = 'none';t2.entry(1,2).Style = {Width('2in')};% To highlight the first rowt.row(1).Style = {BackgroundColor('skyblue') Color('white') Bold() };append(d,t2);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Spurious duplication error when model containing Simulink Functions has a virtual bus output
Description: When a referenced model contains a Simulink Function and also has a virtual bus output, Simulink incorrectly reports an error that this Simulink Function is defined multiple times by the referenced model: Two blocks 'myTopModel/myRefModel', and 'myTopModel/myRefModel' are defining the same function 'myFunction'. Function names must be unique.
Workaround: Configure the referenced model's bus output(s) to be non-virtual. This can be done by selecting Output as nonvirtual bus in parent model in the Signal Attributes tab of the Outport block dialog.

Summary: Masks on Model blocks that reference workspace variables do not always update
Description: Masks are not updated on Model blocks that meet all  of these conditions: Reference base workspace variables Are not at the root of the referencing model Do not have a value set for the 'UserData' parameter  This can result in spurious errors or incorrect model argument values. If the Model block is in the top model, the bug affects only parameter values tuned during runtime, whereas Model blocks in referenced models are affected also during initialization. Beginning in R2014b, this issue affects models running with Fast Restart on. Beginning in R2015b, models running in all modes are affected. This issue causes incorrect model argument values to be passed, resulting in incorrect simulation results. Parameter evaluation errors may be produced concerning Simulink.Parameter  objects.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use one of these approaches:Place the masked Model block at the root of the model which references it.Set a value for Model block 'UserData' parameter.Avoid placing a mask directly on the Model block. For example, masking a subsystem which contains the Model block does not exhibit the bug.

Summary: Status field in the results corrupted with extra characters
Description: In the Results List or Results Details window, the Status field drop-down menu becomes corrupted with extra characters. For example, the "Justify with annotations" status becomes "Justify with annotations efect".

Summary: Release notes do not mention Compatibility Considerations for Initialize Outputs Every Time Chart Wakes Up chart property changes
Description: Starting in R2015b, HDL Coder requires that Stateflow charts have their Initialize Outputs Every Time Chart Wakes Up  chart property enabled in order to generate HDL code. However, the release notes for HDL Coder did not mention this in Compatibility Considerations .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of a fi object with a value of -0.0 may produce unexpected results
Description: When a fi object is constructed with a value of -0.0, the sqrt , bitcmp , and bitsra  functions may not behave as expected. The sqrt  function may emit an error. The bitcmp  may return a large positive value. The bitsra  function will return a negative value when shifting by an amount greater than the word length of the fi object. To trigger this bug, all the following must be true: The fi object is constructed with a value of -0.0. The word length of the fi object is greater than 52 bits.
Workaround: Construct the fi object with a value of 0.0 (positive zero).?

Summary: Incorrect CDF estimation in ksdensity and mvksdensity for more than one-dimensional data
Description: When you compute CDF for more than 1-D data using the ksdensity  or mvksdensity  function, you might get incorrect results when there are data points below the predefined boundary of the current evaluation point.  (The predefined boundary determines the points that contribute to a PDF estimation.)  The CDF estimation incorrectly omits the contributions of these data points. For example: rng(0); X = [0+.5*rand(20,1), 5+2.5*rand(20,1); .75+.25*rand(10,1), 8.75+1.25*rand(10,1)]; f1 = ksdensity(X,[10,100],'function','cdf'); f2 = mvksdensity(X,[10,100],'function','cdf'); The query point in both examples is [10, 100]. The ksdensity  and mvksdensity  functions compute the predefined boundary, which is [9.0145 10.9855] for the first dimension, and [94.8167 105.1833] for the second dimension. The points in X  are all below the lower bound, so their contributions should be included in the CDF estimation, but they are not. Both f1  and f2  should be 1. Instead, the results are both 0.

Summary: Opening a Word document part locks its template file
Description: Opening a Word document part locks its template file. You can open and edit the template in Word and it appears to save your changes. However, Word does not save the changes. If you try to close the template, Word prompts you to save the changes. If you select Save , Word appears to save the template but does not do so. The only way to close the template is to select Don't Save . This script shows the issue. t = mlreportgen.dom.Template('MyTemplate','docx'); append(t,'Open MyTemplate.dotx in MS Word to add content or styles'); close(t); dp = mlreportgen.dom.DocumentPart('docx','MyTemplate.dotx'); % After opening the document part or appending content, % you cannot change MyTemplate.dotx append(dp, 'Hello World!'); d = mlreportgen.dom.Document('MyDocument','docx','MyTemplate'); append(d,dp); close(d);% Try changing, saving, and closing the template. You can% close the template only if you do not save your changes.winopen('MyTemplate.dotx');
Workaround: Delete the document part object after appending it to the document:t = mlreportgen.dom.Template('MyTemplate','docx');append(t,'Open MyTemplate.dotx in MS Word to add content or styles'); close(t); dp = mlreportgen.dom.DocumentPart('docx','MyTemplate.dotx'); % After opening the document part or appending content, % you cannot change MyTemplate.dotx append(dp, 'Hello World!'); d = mlreportgen.dom.Document('MyDocument','docx','MyTemplate'); append(d, dp); close(d);% This is the workaround - delete the document partdelete(dp);% You can now edit and save your template changes in Word.winopen('MyTemplate.dotx');?

Summary: A Unit Delay block with a hybrid input sample time results in assertion during compatibility checks
Description: A Unit Delay block with a hybrid?input sample time acts as a rate transition for the input sample?times that do not match the output sample time. Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checks?report a warning when a Unit Delay block behaves as a rate transition.?However, if the input?sample time is hybrid, compatibility checking results in assertion because the check?expects?only one input sample time.
Workaround: Avoid using a Unit Delay block with a hybrid input sample time. Instead, remodel the Simulink diagram so that?a?Unit Delay block has only one discrete sample time, which is the?same for input and output.

Summary: Generated code for export-function model containing inline variants may contain extra temporary variables
Description: The generated code may contain extra temporary variables for a model that meets the following conditions: The model is set-up to export functions. The Function-call block contains a Variant Source block or a Variant Sink block. The inputs to the Function-call block have different variant conditions than the Function-call block.  Extra temporary variables and their associated data copies can reduce the performance of the generated code.
Workaround: To avoid this bug, try to set-up your model so that the inputs to the Function-call block have the same variant condition as Function-call block.

Summary: Signal Builder block generates index errors when importing data using Replace existing dataset?from the Import File dialog box
Description: When?attempting to import data from file?using the Replace existing?dataset  option, an error message like the following appears in the MATLAB Command Window: Error using sigbldr.controllers.SBFileImportDialogController/handleApplyButtonCallback (line233)Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals. Workaround: As a workaround, make the first signal in the Signal Builder block visible?before using the File >?Import from File command.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator?does not?honor data alignment requirement in generated code when replacing?scalar operations or functions?with custom implementations that require data?alignment
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment requirement when?you use a?code replacement library (CRL)?to replace scalar operations or functions that meet all of these conditions: The?model uses a code replacement library (CRL) to replace operations or functions in the generated code. At least one?scalar operation or function?replacement satisfies?one of these conditions:A?cast, shift, or relational operation is replaced. The replaced operation or function is?inside a?function, and at least one of the inputs or outputs for that function is an I/O variable?that is passed by value.   The corresponding?code replacement?entry specifies an alignment requirement with the AlignmentBoundary  property. If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Code generation may fail for a model that contains the $A token for the?Local temporary variables or Local block output variables parameters
Description: A model may crash or code generation may fail?for a model that contains the $A  token for the Local temporary variables  or Local block output variables  parameters. These parameters are on the Code Generation>Symbols  pane. Code generation fails with an error message similar to the following: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_env.cpp line 292: nextSymbol ! =0"
Workaround: For most?models, you can avoid this bug by removing the $A token from the?naming rules for the Local temporary variables or Local block output variables?parameters.

Summary: Generated code for models containing variant blocks may contain duplicate preprocessor conditionals
Description: For models containing variant blocks, the generated code may contain duplicate preprocessor conditionals (#if ). The duplicate preprocessor conditionals are similar to the following generated code: #if Variant_1 #if Variant_1    <conditional code> #endif  /* Variant_1 */ #endif   In order for the generated code to contain preprocessor conditionals, in the block parameters dialog box for the Variant Subsystem, Variant Source, and Variant Sink blocks, the parameter Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter must be selected. In the Model Variants block parameter dialog box, the Generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter must be selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant block feeding reusable subsystem may lead to incorrect code generation
Description: For a model that meets the following conditions, the code generator might generate incorrect code for the reusable subsystem output function: The model contains at least two instances of the same reusable subsystem. Each reusable subsystem has a Constant block as an input. Each Constant block has a different Constant value  parameter. For the Sample time  parameter, the Constant blocks have the default value of inf . In the Model Configuration parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters  pane, the Default parameter behavior  parameter is set to Inlined  and the Inline invariant signals  parameter is selected.

Summary: Crash after undoing changes made to element selection or assignment in Data Store Read or Write block
Description: If a data store stores array or bus data, MATLAB can crash when you attempt to use the Simulink Editor to undo changes you made to the block parameters with the dialog box still open. For example: Open a Data Store Read or Data Store Write block dialog box. On the Element Selection  or Element Assignment  tab, specify elements to select or assign and click Select>> . Click Apply . Workaround: Instead of clicking Apply, close the block dialog box by clicking OK before trying to undo parameter changes.

Summary: DOM API does not preserve white space at the text level in a PDF document
Description:
The DOM API silently ignores a WhiteSpace format on a Text object when generating the PDF document. For example, the PDF output from this code ignores the WhiteSpace setting: import mlreportgen.dom.*;txtObj = Text(sprintf('hello
 world
  on
   new
   line'));txtObj.WhiteSpace = 'preserve';d = Document('temp','pdf');open(d);append(d,Paragraph(txtObj));close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);
Workaround:
Apply the WhiteSpace setting to the paragraph containing the Text object:import mlreportgen.dom.*;paraObj = Paragraph(sprintf('hello
 world
  on
   new
   line'));paraObj.WhiteSpace = 'preserve';d = Document('temp','pdf');open(d);append(d,paraObj);close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may be incorrect for a MATLAB Function block with variable-size matrix output
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect code for a MATLAB Function block that meets the following conditions: The output data (Y ) is variable-size. Y  has at least 2 dimensions with a maximum size bigger than 1. In the MATLAB code, a statement changes the size of Y and uses the previous value in the same statement.  For example, suppose that a MATLAB Function block contains this code: Y = [Y ; Y]; Y  is a variable size matrix of maximum size [8 8] . The code generator may generate the following incorrect code: ...  model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0] = b_result + c_result;  model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[1] = result;  loop_ub = model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[1];  for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {    loop_ub_0 = model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0];    for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < loop_ub_0; i_0++) {      tmp_data[i_0 + model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0] * i] =        model_B.y[model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0] * i + i_0];    }  }  loop_ub = model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[1];  for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {    loop_ub_0 = model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0];    for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < loop_ub_0; i_0++) {      tmp_data_0[i_0 + model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0] * i] =        model_B.y[model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0] * i + i_0];    }  }  for (i = 0; i < result; i++) {    for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < b_result; i_0++) {      model_B.y[i_0 + model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0] * i] = tmp_data[b_result * i        + i_0];    }  }  for (i = 0; i < result; i++) {    for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < c_result; i_0++) {      model_B.y[(i_0 + b_result) + model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0] * i] =        tmp_data_0[c_result * i + i_0];    }  }... This code is incorrect because it sets the size of the variable model_DW.SFunction_DIMS2[0]  before the first loop. As a result, a copy of Y to a temporary variable accesses the wrong data. The size update should occur between the second and third loops, when the data is copied back to the new dimensions of Y. ? ?
Workaround: In the MATLAB code, introduce a temporary variable such that Y = [Y ; Y] becomestmp = Y; Y = [tmp ; Y];?

Summary: Large parallel pools using MathWorks Hosted License Manager might encounter license error
Description: A parallel pool of 120 or more workers on a MATLAB Distributed Computing Server cluster using MathWorks Hosted License Manager might sporadically error during startup. The parpool fails to start with a License Manager Error 4402, 'Transaction failed' . The likelihood of failure increases as the pool size increases. Example error message: Error using parpool (line 103)Failed to start a parallel pool. (For information in addition to the causing error, validate the profile 'ProfileName' in the Cluster Profile Manager.) Caused by: Error using parallel.internal.pool.InteractiveClient>iThrowWithCause (line 667) Failed to initialize the interactive session. Error using parallel.internal.pool.InteractiveClient>iThrowIfBadParallelJobStatus (line 765) The interactive communicating job errored with the following message: Dispatching the task failed due to an exception thrown on the worker: License checkout failed. License Manager Error 4402 Transaction failed  Troubleshoot this issue by visiting: https://www.mathworks.com/support/lme/R2016a/4402  Diagnostic Information: Feature: MATLAB_Distrib_Comp_Engine License path: Licensing error: 4402,0.   This is causing:  The task failed to start due to a licensing error.
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1374124 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when using an unassigned cell array element in an object property
Description: The generated code will read uninitialized memory when all of these conditions are true: The MATLAB?code contains an assignment in which the left hand side is a property of an object. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized read produced when generating code for coding pattern involving multiple for-loop style while loops that can be combined
Description: MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code with a read of an uninitialized variable for a coding pattern involving: Multiple for-loop style while loops that execute the same number of iterations A while loop induction variable is used outside of a loop  MATLAB Coder contains an optimization that tries to combine multiple loops with the same number of iterations into a single loop. For the described coding pattern, this optimization can incorrectly produce an uninitialized read of a loop induction variable.For an example see function issue_1391944  below: function [out] = issue_1391944()inductionVar1 = 0;while inductionVar1 < 4    inductionVar1 = inductionVar1 + 1;endinductionVar2 = 0;while inductionVar2 < 4    inductionVar2 = inductionVar2+1;endinductionVar3 = 0;while inductionVar3 < 4    inductionVar3 = inductionVar3 + 1;endout = inductionVar1 * inductionVar2 * inductionVar3;end This function produces the below generated code. Note that the three loops have been combined into a single loop:double issue_1391944(void){  int inductionVar2;  int inductionVar1;  inductionVar2 = inductionVar1;  for (inductionVar1 = 0; inductionVar1 < 4; inductionVar1++) {  }  return inductionVar1 * inductionVar2 * inductionVar1;} The issue can be seen prior to the loop. The uninitialized value of inductionVar1 is written into inductionVar2 and this value is used to compute the return value. Instead the following code should have been generated:double issue_1391944(void){  int inductionVar2;  int inductionVar1;  for (inductionVar1 = 0; inductionVar1 < 4; inductionVar1++) {  }  inductionVar2 = inductionVar1;  return inductionVar1 * inductionVar2 * inductionVar1;} This issue affects the following code generation workflows: Code generation using MATLAB Coder only Code generation from a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function block, MATLAB System block, or MATLAB Function in Stateflow

Summary: Model Advisor may incorrectly report a model as being incompatible for Simulink Design Verifier
Description: The Model Advisor check "Check compatibility with Simulink Design Verifier" will report a model as being incompatible if warnings are generated during the compatibility check. However, checking for compatibility using the menu option Analysis ->Design Verifier ->Check Compatibility  will report the model as compatible while generating warnings in the Diagnostic Viewer.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might propagate sample times incorrectly when the design model contains function-triggered model references
Description: Simulink Design Verifier inlines model references when you run design error detection or test generation analyses. While inlining model references, Design Verifier also sets sample times for the ports of inlined sub-systems. This step resolves sample time propagation for model references and atomic sub-systems. When the design model has function-triggered model references with inports but no outports, the sample time propagation incorrectly propagates values. This causes errors in Design Verifier analysis.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may be incorrect for a model that contains chained Merge blocks that span reusable subsystem boundaries
Description: When Merge blocks cross reusable subsystem1  boundaries, the generated code may fail to pass the output signal to the function call resulting in incorrect code. The code generator may generate this code for a model that meets the following conditions. Note that the subsystem names in parentheses refer to the names in the example model attached to this EBR. The model contains a reusable subsystem (ss_bottom ) that contains multiple blocks that connect to a Mux block. The outport of the Mux block connects to the outport of ss_bottom . A reusable subsystem (ss_middle ) contains ss_bottom  and a Merge block. The outport of the Merge block connects to the outport of ss_middle . The above Merge block connects to another Merge block in a parent subsystem. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization>Signals and Parameters  pane, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Individual arguments .  The attached example model and images and the code snippet below show a case in which the generated code is uncompilable because g1388596_example_B  is only accessible in the local scope. For some models, g1388596_example_B  may be defined globally. For those models, the generated code compiles but could possiby give wrong answers. void g1388596_example_SS_middle(real32_T rtu_AThreshStat) {  if (rtu_AThreshStat == 1.0F) {g1388596_example_SS_bottom(&g1388596_example_B->Merge[0],&g1388596_example_B->Merge[1]);   }  }void g1388596_example_step(RT_MODEL_g1388596_example *const g1388596_example_M,  real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN1, real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN3, real_T  g1388596_example_Y_RootOutPort[2]){...  if (!(g1388596_example_U_DIN1 == 1.0F)) {g1388596_example_SS_middle(g1388596_example_U_DIN3);   }...  }  The generated code does not contain the output of SS_middle  in its function call. The definition of SS_middle  instead refers to a variable (g1388596_example_B->Merge ) that is only accessible inside the top level model. The following code snippet shows the correct generated code: void g1388596_example_SS_middle(real32_T rtu_AThreshStat, real_T rty_MidOutPort[2])  {  if (rtu_AThreshStat == 1.0F) { g1388596_example_SS_bottom(&rty_MidOutPort[0], &rty_MidOutPort[1]);   }  }  void g1388596_example_step(RT_MODEL_g1388596_example *const g1388596_example_M,  real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN1, real32_T g1388596_example_U_DIN3, real_T  g1388596_example_Y_RootOutPort[2]){  ...  if (!(g1388596_example_U_DIN1 == 1.0F)) {g1388596_example_SS_middle(g1388596_example_U_DIN3,  g1388596_example_B->Merge);   }  ...}  The correct code contains the output of ss_middle  (that is, rty_MidOutPort ) in its function call. 1  For a subsystem, the code format may be reusable if on the Block Parameters dialog box, the Code Generation>Function packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function  or Auto .
Workaround: The following are some possible workarounds:For multiple Merge blocks in a chain, only the last one in the chain should connect to a reusable subsystem outport.Insert a nonvirtual block in between the Merge blocks. Note that this workaround causes extra memory consumption since previously all the inputs to the Merge block share the same variable.Do not connect multiple blocks to a Mux block and then connect this Mux block directly to a chain of Merge blocks.Change subsystem packaging to inline or nonreusable.

Summary: Simulation error when piece of structure or array of structures used to set value of structured model argument
Description: Simulating a model generates an error if all of these conditions are true: The model (for example, myTopModel ) references another model (myModel ). myModel  references a third model (myRefModel ). myRefModel  configures a Simulink.Parameter  object (myArg ) in the model workspace as a model argument. The data type of myArg  is a Simulink.Bus  object. In myModel , the value of the model argument (set in the Model block that references myRefModel ) is set by a field of a structure or an element of an array of structures. For example, the argument value is myStruct.myField  or myStructs(2) . myStruct  or myStructs  is a Simulink.Parameter  object. The data type of the parameter object is a Simulink.Bus  object. The model argument and the model argument value use the same bus object as the data type.  When you simulate myTopModel , the error message is similar to: An error occurred during simulation of Model block 'myTopModel/Model'.Caused by:Invalid setting in 'myModel/Model' for parameter 'myArg'.
Workaround: In the model workspace for myRefModel, set the DataType property of myArg to struct.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when building a Simulink model that contains Variant blocks with nested Simulink.Variant objects
Description: Building a Simulink model might crash MATLAB if the following conditions are true:?  The models contains Variant blocks such as?Variant Subsystems, Variant Sources, Variant Sinks or Model Variants The condition of a Simulink.Variant  object includes other Simulink.Variant  objects  Consider a model that contains a Variant Subsystem block with two variant choices, Variant1  and Variant2 .? Variant1  is a Simulink.Variant  object with condition 'Variant1a && Variant1b' : Variant1 = Simulink.Variant('Variant1a && Variant1b');? Summary: Incorrect Resource Pool block behavior when using Entity Replicator or Entity Multicast blocks
Description: Using Entity Replicator or Entity Multicast block with entities that have acquired resources (with the Resource Pool block) causes incorrect duplication of resources. This duplication may lead to the use of more resources than are available in the system. To see if your model experiences this issue, check if the output value of the Amount in use, u  statistic on the Resource Pool block becomes negative.
Workaround: Explicitly release resources using the Resource Releaser block before passing entities through an Entity Multicast or an Entity Replicator block.

Summary: Retrieving ROS time repeatedly can lead to MATLAB error or crash
Description: Calling the rostime('now') function repeatedly, for example in a loop, can lead to a MATLAB error and/or a MATLAB crash. Depending on your machine configuration, this usually occurs after several thousand calls to rostime('now'). This issue affects all platforms, but on Mac machines the effect is seen earlier compared to other platforms. When the problem occurs, MATLAB will display an error message with the text "java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: unable to create new native thread" and ROS functionality will stop working after this. In some cases, MATLAB can crash as well.
Workaround: Instead of calling rostime('now') repeatedly, we suggest that you create a time object once and then retrieve the current time from this object.?For example, if you want to retrieve the current time repeatedly in a loop, you should replace the following code:? ?for i = 1:1000 ?? ? ? ?currentTime = rostime('now') ??? ? ? ?.... ? ?endwith the following code:?? ? ?? t = robotics.ros.internal.Time([]);? ? for i = 1:1000? ? ? ?currentTime = t.CurrentTime; ?? ? ??....?? ? end

Summary: In perspective projection, 3D objects are clipped when the camera position is close to the object.
Description: If Projection property on the Axes is set to 'perspective', for a certain values of CameraPosition property, 3D objects are clipped.[x,y,z] = sphere; surf(x,y,z) % x, y and z are in the range [-1,1] axis([-10, 40, -25, 25, -25, 25]); ax = gca;ax.Projection = 'perspective'; ax.CameraViewAngle = 40; ax.CameraPosition = [10 0 0]; ax.CameraTarget = [ 0 0 0]; ax.CameraUpVector = [ 0 0 1]; drawnow;ax.CameraPosition = [5.8 0 0]; % camera is still 4.8 units away from the objectThis occurs due to incorrectly computed near plane values.MATLAB computes near/far planes for every scene in order to maximize the depth resolution in the scene by placing near and far planes as close to the scene (3d objects) as possible. In this case, MATLAB computes a near value that is too close to the camera and then clamps it to a small threshold causing clipping artifact.

Summary: Indenting a PDF list can double indentation of its entries
Description: Indenting a PDF list can double indentation of its entries. For example, this script indents the list by 0.4 inches and its entries by 0.8 inches. import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = mlreportgen.dom.Document('indent_list','pdf');ul = append(rpt,{'one','two','three'});ul.Style = {OuterMargin('0.4in','0in')};ol = append(rpt,OrderedList({'one','two','three'}));ol.Style = {OuterMargin('0.4in','0in')};close(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);
Workaround: Use a template-defined style to indent your list:import mlreportgen.dom.*% Create template-based style.css = { ...'' ...'ul.MyListStyle {' ...'    margin-left: 0.4in;' ...'}' ...};format = 'pdf';templateName = [format 'Template'];templateFile = [templateName '.' format(1:3) 'tx'];mlreportgen.dom.Document.createTemplate(templateName,format);unzipTemplate(templateFile);fileID = fopen([templateName '/stylesheets/root.css'],'w');for line = css    fprintf(fileID,'%s\n',line{1});endfclose(fileID);delete(templateFile);zipTemplate(templateFile,templateName);% Use stylerpt = mlreportgen.dom.Document('indent_list',format,templateName);ul = append(rpt,{'one','two','three'});ul.StyleName = 'MyListstyle';ol = append(rpt,OrderedList({'one','two','three'}));ol.StyleName = 'MyListstyle';close(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);

Summary: The DOM API does not honor styles defined only in PDF or HTML template files used only by document parts appended to a document
Description: The DOM API honors only styles defined in a PDF or HTML document's main template file. It does not honor styles defined only in template files used only by document parts appended to the document.
Workaround: Define all styles used by a PDF or HTML document, including those used by document parts, in the document's main template file.

Summary: PDF document part templates that do not specify a page layout crash MATLAB
Description: Opening a PDF document part whose template does not include a page layout element crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: Include a page layout element at the beginning of each PDF document part template used by your report.

Summary: movmedian(X,K) returns the wrong answer for large K when X is an integer type
Description: The movmedian  function returns the wrong answer when the input array is an integer data type and the window size K  is larger than 12.
Workaround: To work around this behavior, cast the input array to type single or double (for example, movmedian(double(X),K)).

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might fail on design models with root inports of virtual bus type when the model involves model references.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier inlines model reference blocks before analyzing design model with references. In cases where the root inport of the design model is of virtual bus type and passes the signal to a model block as non-virtual bus, the inliner fails to add signal conversions. Virtual buses constructed through a bus creator and model references inside atomic subsystems with virtual bus ports have no such issues. When a signal passes into a model directly from the root inport of virtual bus type, Simulink Design Verifier sometimes throws an error.
Workaround: For cases where Simulink Design Verifier analyses fails as described in the Bug Description, convert the affected inport bus to non-virtual type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible when an Assignment block and a Unit Delay block are in the same For iterator subsystem
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect code for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Iterator subsystem.  The For Iterator subsystem contains an Assignment block and a Unit Delay block. In the Assignment block parameters dialog box, the Index Option  parameter is set to Index vector (port)  or Starting index (port).  The?Initialize output (Y)  parameter is set to Specify size for each dimension in table.  In the Unit?Delay block parameters dialog box, the Initial condition  parameter has initial values.  In the generated code, the Unit Delay block output may?be incorrect. For example, the Unit Delay block output may be partially updated at each simulation step.?

Summary: fplot can show empty plots
Description: In some cases, the? fplot ?function displays emptyaxes with both the x -axis and y -axis limits ranging from 0 to 1.
Workaround: You might be able to work around this behavior by tweakingthe x-axis range. For example, try fplot(@f,[2 3.1]) instead of fplot(@f,[2 3]).Otherwise, set the axis range using the axis?function, for example, axis([0 2 -5 5])?(i.e., [xmin xmax ymin ymax]).

Summary: DOM API incorrectly indents a table in a PDF report
Description: The DOM API indents a table's entries twice as far as it indents the table, resulting in misaligned entries. For example, this script indents a table 1.5 inches and its entries 3 inches: import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('output','pdf');append(rpt,Heading1('Outer margin is applied to fo:table-cell entries')); tbl = append(rpt, magic(10)); tbl.Style = {OuterMargin('1.5in','0','0','0')}; tbl.TableEntriesStyle = { Width('0.25in') }; tbl.RowSep = 'solid'; tbl.ColSep = 'solid';close(rpt); winopen(rpt.OutputPath); ?
Workaround: Left indent the table's entries by the same amount that you right indent the table:import mlreportgen.dom.* rpt = Document('output','pdf'); append(rpt,Heading1('Outer margin is applied to fo:table-cell entries')); tbl = append(rpt,magic(10)); tbl.Style = {OuterMargin('1.5in','0','0','0') }; tbl.TableEntriesStyle = {Width('0.25in'), OuterMargin('-1.5in','0','0','0')}; tbl.RowSep = 'solid'; tbl.ColSep = 'solid'; close(rpt); winopen(rpt.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of a fi object as a block parameter with a value of -0.0 may produce incorrect Accelerator mode simulation results
Description: When a fi object constructed with a value of -0.0 is used as a block parameter, Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator mode simulation may give incorrect results. To trigger this bug, all the following must be true: The fi object is constructed with a value of -0.0 The word length of the fi object is greater than 52 bits and not a multiple of 32 (e.g. 64 bit fi objects are not affected by this bug)
Workaround: Follow one of the two workarounds:Construct the fi object with a value of 0.0 (positive zero)Use a fi object with a word length that is a multiple of 32 (e.g. instead of 62 bit word length, use 64 bit word length)Do not use a fi object as a parameter value (most blocks allow a floating-point parameter value and provide fields to specify the parameter type)
