Summary: Simulink Web View does not report on some block/signal/object parameters
Description: Simulink Web View does not report on some block/signal/object parameters. For example, Simulink Web View does not report on these signal parameters:  SignalNameFromLabel   MustResolveToSignal   ShowPropagatedSignal
Workaround: You can visit Bug Report ID 1395948 to download a patch that addresses this issue.

Summary: Link error in SIL or PIL simulation
Description: On a Windows system, when the MATLAB current folder name has a lowercase drive letter, a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation can fail with a link error. If you use the Microsoft Visual C++ compiler for building MEX files, you see: % LINK : fatal error LNK1104: cannot open file If you use the MinGW-w64 compiler, you see: ... x86_64-w64-mingw32/bin/ld.exe:cannot open output file In a MATLAB session, the failure can occur in the second or a subsequent SIL or PIL simulation of a model. The following example produces this error: folder = tempname;mkdir(folder)cd(lower(folder)) % Forces lower case drive letterrtwdemo_sil_topmodelsim('rtwdemo_sil_topmodel')set_param('rtwdemo_sil_topmodel', 'CodeProfilingInstrumentation', 'on')sim('rtwdemo_sil_topmodel') % Fails with link error ?
Workaround: Ensure that the MATLAB current folder has an uppercase drive letter:upperPwd = pwd;upperPwd(1) = upper(upperPwd(1));cd ..cd(upperPwd)Alternatively, before each SIL or PIL simulation, run the following command:clear mex

Summary: Segmentation violation or incorrect error message when building AUTOSAR model that uses Signal Invalidation block with nonscalar data and schema version below 4.0
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR model fails without displaying a useful error message when both of the following conditions exist:  The model contains a Signal Invalidation block with a data input configured for nonscalar data, such as vector or matrix data. The AUTOSAR schema version configured for the model is lower than 4.0.   Under these conditions, building the model should fail with a message noting a software limitation. Instead, the build fails with a segmentation violation (R2015b) or an incorrect error message (R2016a).
Workaround: Embedded Coder has a limitation that it cannot generate code for a Signal Invalidation block configured for nonscalar data, if the AUTOSAR schema version is lower than 4.0.To work around this limitation,?set the AUTOSAR schema version to 4.0 or higher.

Summary: Various MATLAB GUI elements are not responsive when the input command is waiting to return.
Description: MATLAB GUI elements, such as the figure window toolstrip, cannot be used while waiting for user input at the MATLAB command line. The toolstrip does not perform the desired action until the input  command returns. For example, the following commands demonstrate the issue: x = 1:10; y = 2*x + 3;plot(x,y)input('Enter any number '); While input  is active, the zoom tool, hand tool, or any other item from the toolstrip does not work as expected.

Summary: Initial values specified for differential variables change unexpectedly during initialization
Description: Certain Simscape components incorrectly assign the initialization priority of None  to some differential variables. As a result, it is possible that the initial value targets specified for these variables are not satisfied during initialization. This issue affects the Constant Volume Hydraulic Chamber block in Foundation library, Vehicle Body block in Simscape Driveline,as well as any blocks deriving from those components or using them internally (such as Pipelines or Low-Pressure Blocks in Simscape Fluids). This bug can also affect custom components that use the der  operator within the let  section (but not within the in  part) of the let-in  expressions in equations. ?
Workaround: Change log:July 28, 2016: attachment_1379362_16a_2016-07-28.zip: initial version

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from Simulink blocks or MATLAB functions that use hypot functions with C standard math library C99 (ISO) or a code replacement library derived from that math library might produce results that differ from simulation results
Description: Code generated with Simulink Coder or MATLAB Coder might produce results that differ from simulation results if all of the following conditions exist: You configure the code generator to use the C standard math library ISO/IEC 9899:1999 (C99 (ISO)) or a code replacement library derived from that standard math library. You generate code from Simulink blocks or MATLAB functions that use hypot  or hypotf  function. Values of input arguments in the calls to hypot  or hypotf  are Inf  and NaN  or NaN  and Inf .

Summary: MATLAB might crash when an attribute of type int64 or uint64 is used in Entity Generator block
Description: When using an Entity Generator block to define an entity type, MATLAB may crash if you specify an attribute initial value of type int64 or uint64 in the "Entity Type" tab of the block dialog.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated C/C++ code for a recursive MATLAB function inside Stateflow Chart may be incorrect if the function contains a large variable
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect C/C++ code for a Stateflow Chart that meets the following conditions: The Stateflow Chart contains a MATLAB function and a graphical function. The MATLAB function calls the graphical function which calls the same MATLAB function. The MATLAB function contains a local variable that is large enough to exceed the value of the TLC variable MaxStackVariableSize . For a Windows target, the default value of this variable is 4096 bytes.  ?

Summary: Models with 3DoF Animation or 6DoF Animation blocks issues an error during update diagram
Description: When performing an Update Diagram action on a model?that contains 3DoF Animation or 6DoF Animation blocks, the following error can appear in the Diagnostic Viewer: ? ? ? Simulation Snapshot Manager not available for model 'model_name', is the model compiled?  This same error can occur when doing other partial compilation tasks, such as:  Displaying sample time colors  Displaying signal dimensions or datatypes Using the Model command interface to the simulation  The error does not interrupt the partial compilation process -- the Update Diagram completes even though an error message appears. The error does not appear during simulation.

Summary: To Workspace block does not log all signals in multirate virtual buses
Description: When you log a multirate virtual bus signal using the To Workspace block, the logged data might not include data for one or more signals in the bus.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from Simulink blocks or MATLAB functions that calculate remainder after division with standard math library C99(ISO) or code replacement library derived from that math library might produce results that differ from simulation results
Description: Code generated with Simulink Coder or MATLAB Coder might produce results that differ from simulation results if all of the following conditions exist: You configure the code generator to use the C standard math library ISO/IEC 9899:1999 (C99 (ISO)) or a code replacement library derived from that standard math library. You generate code for Simulink blocks or MATLAB functions that calculate the remainder after division. For example, if you generate code for the Simulink Rem or Repeating Sequence block or the MATLAB rem  function. Values of input arguments are not integers.

Summary: plotSlice method for LinearModel and other classes may fail
Description: The plotSlice  method operates on fitted regression objects, such as the object with class name LinearModel  produced by the fitlm  command. Sometimes this method may fail with an error message, or it may produce a plot that is incomplete. This problem has been observed when the fitted model is created from matrices (rather than tables), and when the model either contains categorical predictors or it uses only a subset of the matrix columns as predictors.

Summary: App Designer editor becomes slow and unresponsive
Description: The App Designer editor can become unresponsive when there are several coding alerts, or when your code has more than 1000 lines.
Workaround: You can minimize coding alerts in one of the following ways:Fix the coding problems flagged in the alerts.Disable the coding alerts by clearing the Enable app coding alerts check box. You must clear this check box each time you open the app in App Designer. Once you disable the alerts, you must find and fix the coding problems yourself.If you have more than 1000 lines of code, consider breaking your code up into separate files by following these steps:1. Move your utility functions into a separate file that defines a new class. Define your utility functions as methods on that class. The methods must accept an object as the first argument and an app as the second argument. Specify any additional input arguments after those first two arguments. For example:classdef MyAppHelper    methods       function myfun1(obj,app,x)            disp(x);       end       function val = myfun2(obj,app,y)            val = y + 1;       end    endend 2. Next, add a property to your app called Helper.3. In your app's StartupFcn callback, add this command: app.Helper = MyAppHelper();4. Modify all instances in your app code that call one of the utility functions. The first input argument must be the property you defined in Step 2 (for example, app.Helper). The second input argument must be the app object. Any additional input arguments your function uses must follow those first two input arguments. For example:myfun1(app.Helper,app,10);

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector reports "Partially verified" status for commented out blocks?in model
Description: A commented out block?in a model?introduces ambiguity in the dataflow semantics of the Simulink diagram.?Blocks that feed input to or receive input?from a commented out block need to be grounded or terminated during model simulation. This creates a gap between the graphical and simulation list of blocks.? As a result, if you comment out a block, Simulink Code Inspector returns "Partially verified" for the inspection.
Workaround: Delete commented out blocks from the Simulink?model.

Summary: Incorrect symbolic solution of a polynomial system
Description: The bug causes the solve  function in MATLAB&#0174 and MuPAD&#0174 Notebook to return an incorrect result when solving systems of rational, non-linear equations in several variables with constant coefficients, under certain exceptional circumstances. When the same system is solved using vpasolve , then vpasolve  does not return a solution and instead warns that a numerical instability is encountered.
Workaround: Substitute the solution you get back into the equations to verify it. If it does not satisfy the equations, try the following:Change the order of variables.Change a numerical coefficient slightly, such as changing 1 to 1.00001.Replace a numerical coefficient by a symbolic variable, then solve the system, and substitute the numerical coefficient back.

Summary: Generated arxml code missing VARIABLE-ACCESS node corresponding to read of AUTOSAR inter-runnable variable
Description: The arxml  file generated from an AUTOSAR model might incorrectly describe a read of an inter-runnable variable (IRV) if all  of the following conditions are true: The connected runnables are modeled using function-call inports (exported function modeling style). The subsystem inport corresponding to the read of the IRV is latched. The IRV is connected to a Switch block. Model option Conditional input branch execution  (ConditionallyExecuteInputs ) is selected.  Under these conditions, arxml  export fails to generate a VARIABLE-ACCESS node describing the read of the IRV.
Workaround: To work around this bug, clear the option Conditional input branch execution. For example:set_param(bdroot,'ConditionallyExecuteInputs','off');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when using fiaccel for an operation that indexes a matrix, transposes the result, and writes to the same matrix
Description: fiaccel  might produce incorrect results for an operation that indexes a matrix, transposes the result, and then writes to the same matrix. The generated MEX might incorrectly write the transposed data before reading it in the next loop iteration. Example 1  A function with the form P = fcn(P) contains one of the patterns: function P = fcn(P) P = K .* P(I,J)'; P = K * P(I,J)';   P = P(I,J)'; P = [K, P(I,J)'];  P is non-scalar, and the result of P(I,J) is non-scalar. K can be non-scalar or scalar. The .* operator in the first example can be any element-wise operator. Example 2  A function with the form Y = fcn(X) contains any of the above patterns followed by the assignment of the result to an output variable: function Y = foo(X) X = X(I,J)'; Y = X; For this case, during code generation, MATLAB Coder converts Y = foo(X)  to Y = foo(Y ). This issue also occurs when you generate code using MATLAB Coder. See Bug Report ID 1369501
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1369501 to download a patch that addresses this bug.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results for modeling pattern or operation that indexes a matrix, transposes the result, and writes to the same matrix
Description: Simulation or code generation might produce incorrect results for a modeling pattern or operation that indexes a matrix, transposes the result, and then writes to the same matrix. This issue might occur in any of these cases: The operation occurs in a function such as a MATLAB Function block, Stateflow Chart, Discrete Event Chart in SimEvents, and Simulink Function block. You run a model with this operation using accelerator or rapid accelerator mode. You use Simulink Coder to generate standalone C/C++ code for that operation.  You can also encounter this issue when using MATLAB Coder to generate MEX or standalone C/C++ code, or fiaccel  to accelerate fixed-point code. See Bug Report ID 1369501  and Bug Report ID 1371943 .
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1369501 to download a patch that addresses this bug.

Summary: Windows crashes when using winvideo on a Surface Pro 4
Description: If you use the winvideo  adaptor on a Microsoft Surface Pro 4 to acquire images using the built-in front and rear cameras, the Windows OS may crash with this message: DRIVER_POWER_STATE_FAILURE

Summary: Table of contents missing and autonumbers not updated in Word report
Description: The table of contents might be missing from Word reports generated by the Report Explorer using the Word (from template)  output option. This issue occurs if: You do not select the View report after generation  option on the Report Explorer's Report Options  dialog box before you generate the report You generate the report using the View report after generation  option, close the report without saving it or after saving it without changes, and then reopen it  If you use a template that contains Word autonumber (seq) fields, for example, to number sections, the seq fields are also not updated in these cases.
Workaround: You can generate the report's table of contents and update seq fields as follows:Open your report in Microsoft Word.Press Ctrl+A to select the report's contents.Press F9 to update any seq fields included in your report.Press F9 to generate the table of contents.In Word's Update Table of Contents dialog box, select Update entire table.Click OK.Save your report.Note: The Report Explorer's default conversion template does not use seq fields. If your report uses the default template or a template that does not contain seq fields, press F9 only once.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized size vector in generated code
Description: For some cases of multiple complete assignments to an array, the generated code contains an unitialized size vector. Your C compiler might warn that a variable is uninitialized. This issue can occur when two different parts of the code assign all elements of an array to different values. For example: function o = myfcn(x,n)assert(n==5);y = zeros(2,n);for k = 1:size(y,1)    y(k,:) = x(k,:);endo = int32(y); This issue can occur for any code generation product.
Workaround: Download the attached p-file and install it in a folder on the MATLAB path.Rewrite the code to call the p-file before the assignment in the part of the code that performs the second assignment. Here is the modified code for the previous example:function o=fcn(x,n)assert(n==5);y=zeros(2,n);for k = 1:size(y,1)   if nonConstTrueFixUpToR2016b()     y(k,:)=x(k,:);   endendo=int32(y);

Summary: Error when running the pcfitplane help example.
Description: If you run the example using your own data, you may get an error. When findPointsInROI  method is called, the first argument should be remainPtCloud , instead of ptCloud . Index exceeds matrix dimensions.   Error in pointCloud/subsetImpl (line 565)                      loc = this.Location(indices, :);   Error in pointCloud/select (line 442)              [loc, c, nv] = this.subsetImpl(indices);   Error in vision.internal.ransac.initializeRansacModel (line 15)      pc = select(ptCloud, sampleIndices);   Error in pcfitplane (line 125)      vision.internal.ransac.initializeRansacModel(ptCloud, sampleIndices, sampleSize);
Workaround: Change the line of code:    sampleIndices = findPointsInROI(ptCloud, roi);to the following:    sampleIndices = findPointsInROI(remainPtCloud, roi);

Summary: Crash when modifying entry in circular hierarchy of referenced dictionaries
Description: When you?modify an entry in a Simulink data dictionary (.sldd  file), MATLAB can crash if?a circular dependency?exists in referenced dictionaries.  For example, suppose targetDict.sldd  references immediateChild.sldd , which ?references bottomDict.sldd . If you configure bottomDict.sldd  to reference targetDict.sldd , the dictionary hierarchy contains a circular dependency.?When you modify an entry in targetDict.sldd , MATLAB can crash.
Workaround: Eliminate the circular dependency by rearranging dictionary references and moving shared entries as low in the dictionary hierarchy as necessary.

Summary: Report Explorer uses a serif instead of a monospace font to render MATLAB code in PDF reports

Workaround: Create a custom PDF template that uses Courier as the template style sheet's monospace font:Open the Report Explorer by entering setedit at the MATLAB command prompt.In the Report Explorer, select Tools > Edit Document Conversion Template.In the template browser, select Default PDF Template.In the template dialog box, click Copy template.Use the file browser to save a copy of the template under a new name on the MATLAB path.In the template browser, select the new template. In the template dialog box, click Open style sheet.In the template editor, replace all occurrences of rg-monospace with Courier.Save the template.Use the new template to format reports containing MATLAB code.

Summary: Performance regression when programmatically advancing interactive Simulink simulations through the Pause command
Description: Pressing the Play  button on a Simulink model window starts an interactive simulation of that model. Interactive simulation advances when MATLAB is otherwise idle and using the pause  command is a way to advance the interactive simulation by pausing other MATLAB tasks. However, if you try to do this, the simulation does not advance for most of the requested duration of the pause, resulting in a performance regression. For example:  mdl = 'my_model'; open_system(mdl); set_param(mdl,'SimulationCommand','Start')while (1)% pause for 2 seconds to let the simulation advancepause(2);if strcmp(get_param(mdl,'SimulationStatus'), 'stopped')% if the simulation is done, as indicated by SimulationStatus,%  break out of the loopbreak;enddo_something_else_while_simulation_runs();end  Only the pause  command is affected. There is no performance regression if you do either of these actions: Press the play button and let the simulation run without pausing in MATLAB. Invoke the sim  command to simulate a model.
Workaround: Instead of using the pause command, you can run a simulation so that MATLAB is blocked during the simulation (when you do not want other commands to run while simulation is running) using the sim command:simOut = sim(mdl); To run MATLAB code while the simulation is running, use the sim command in conjunction with a timer, so that Simulink executes your code on a periodic time interval. For example:tmr = timer;tmr.Period = 2; %timer callback period in secondstmr.ExecutionMode = 'fixedRate';tmr.TimerFcn = @(timerObject, thisEvent)disp('this will show up while SIM runs');  % start the timer.start(tmr);% launch the simulation. The timer will trigger the callback function during simulation  simOut = sim(mdl); % delete the timer object once the simulation is done.stop(tmr); delete(tmr);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for an operation that indexes a matrix, transposes the result, and writes to the same matrix
Description: Code generation might produce incorrect results for an operation that indexes a matrix, transposes the result, and then writes to the same matrix. The generated code might incorrectly write the transposed data before reading it in the next loop iteration. Example 1  A function with the form P = fcn(P) contains one of the patterns: function P = fcn(P) P = K .* P(I,J)'; P = K * P(I,J)';   P = P(I,J)'; P = [K, P(I,J)'];  P is non-scalar, and the result of P(I,J) is non-scalar. K can be non-scalar or scalar. The .* operator in the first example can be any element-wise operator. Example 2  A function with the form Y = fcn(X) contains any of the above patterns followed by the assignment of the result to an output variable: function Y = foo(X) X = X(I,J)'; Y = X; For this case, during code generation, MATLAB Coder converts Y = foo(X)  to Y = foo(Y ).

Summary: False dead code after a cast involving pointers
Description: Sometimes, casting a pointer can cause a false dead code result. The problem can be reproduced with this code snippet:  signed short tab[2]; unsigned short x = 32768;  tab[0] = 0 ;  tab[1] = *(signed short*) &x;  assert(tab[1] == -32767) ; // <- dead code  In this example, the assert statement is reachable. However, the preceding pointer casts to unsigned short and signed short stop the propagation of colors in Polyspace and incorrectly marked the assert statement as unreachable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for an integer parameter with a custom storage class of Define or ImportedDefine may cause arithmetic overflow
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a Simulink.Parameter  that meets the following conditions:  The parameter has a Fixed point  or Built in  integer data type. The parameter has a Custom Storage Class of Define  or ImportedDefine . The parameter specifies a symbolic dimension and is used elsewhere in the model as part of an arithmetic expression.  In the incorrect generated code, the C compiler may treat the value of the parameter?as a different data type than is?in the Simulink model. Such a data type mismatch may cause arithmetic operations to yield incorrect results. For example, a parameter of type unsigned short  in a model may be an unsigned int  in the generated C code when it should be cast to a signed int . As a result, signed int  values may be incorrectly treated as unsigned values. When a negative int  value is wrongly interpreted as a large unsigned int  value, integer overflow occurs.
Workaround: If you have your own TLC file for the ImportedDefine or Define custom storage class, generate a data type cast to explicitly convert integers of mismatched type to the correct integer data type used in model. Perform the cast either in a?#define?statement or in the expression where the variable is referenced.

Summary: Sharing warning message refers to non-existent "Default parameter behavior" optimization configuration parameter
Description: When your design tries to share atomic subsystems, if you do not set the 'Default parameter behavior' to 'Inlined', HDL Coder emits the following warning: HDL code from identical atomic subsystems cannot be reused unless the optimization configuration parameter 'Default parameter behavior' is set to 'Inlined'; code sharing is disabled. If you do not have Simulink Coder, the 'Optimizations' tab in the Model Configuration Parameters may not contain the 'Default parameter behavior' parameter.
Workaround: To set the 'Default parameter behavior' to 'Inlined' and act on the above warning, in the command-line, use set_param(modelname, 'InlineParams', 'on').

Summary: Using run-time parameters in Simscape may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Several blocks in Simscape add-on products mistakenly let you specify certain block parameters as run-time configurable, while in reality they can only be compile-time configurable. If you enable the Simscape run-time parameter setting and specify any of the affected parameters as run-time configurable, MATLAB may crash. The following is the full list of affected blocks and the affected parameters in each block, listed by product. Product name: Simscape Fluids  Double-Acting Actuator (TL) Environment pressure specification Environment pressure Fluid dynamic compressibility Initial liquid temperature in chamber A Initial liquid temperature in chamber B Initial liquid pressure in chamber A Initial liquid pressure in chamber B  Single-Acting Actuator (TL) Environment pressure specification Environment pressure Fluid dynamic compressibility Initial liquid temperature Initial liquid pressure  Double-Acting Hydraulic Cylinder Piston area A Piston area B Piston stroke Piston initial distance from cap A Dead volume A Dead volume B Chamber A initial pressure Chamber B initial pressure Specific heat ratio Cylinder orientation  Double-Acting Rotary Actuator Actuator displacement Shaft stroke Shaft initial angle Dead volume A Dead volume B Specific heat ratio Actuator orientation  Single-Acting Hydraulic Cylinder Piston area Initial distance between piston and cap Dead volume Chamber initial pressure Specific heat ratio Cylinder orientation  Single-Acting Rotary Actuator Actuator displacement Shaft initial angle Dead volume Specific heat ratio Actuator orientation  Product name: Simscape Driveline  Piston Engine Number of pistons Offset angle vector Cylinder bore Piston stroke Piston rod length Number of strokes per cycle Pressure parameterization Crank angle vector Throttle vector Crank velocity Summary: Compiling generated C/C++ source files using Xcode 7.x takes much longer time than in previous versions
Description: On MAC, compiling generated C/C++ source files using Xcode 7.x takes much longer than in previous versions of Xcode due to a slow command in the generated makefile used to compile the code. The following features and products are affected on the MAC: Simulink accelerator and rapid accelerator simulation modes MATLAB Coder Simulink Coder Embedded Coder
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file: Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:March 18, 2016: attachment_1365364_15a_maci64_2016-03-18.zip: initial version, works on R2015a maci64 onlyMarch 18, 2016: attachment_1365364_15b_maci64_2016-03-18.zip: initial version, works on R2015b maci64 onlyMarch 18, 2016: attachment_1365364_16a_maci64_2016-03-18.zip: initial version, works on R2016a maci64 only

Summary: Error message occurs on Windows stating that the build has failed
Description: The build fails on Windows with an error message similar to the following: NMAKE : fatal error U1073: don't know how to make 'C:\Program'  Stop.  This can occur if MATLAB is installed into a path with spaces, which is the case for the default install location of: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R20YYx . The Simulink Coder build process normally accomodates the limitation of makefile utilitites with respect to spaces in paths by populating the makefile variable ALT_MATLAB_ROOT  with the Windows short filename (SFN), usually C:\PROGRA~1 . However, it is becoming more common for Windows systems to be configured with the support of SFN disabled when using the New Technology File System (NTFS) or for Windows to be configured to use the newer Resilient File System (ReFS), which does not support SFN. Note: The Windows short filename (SFN) is sometimes called the 8.3 filename.
Workaround: Workarounds include any of these:Re-install MATLAB into a path without any spaces.Manually map a network drive to the "MATLAB" folder of the installation that has spaces in the path. For example if you installed MATLAB at c:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2016aRun the Windows command: net use * "\\<computer_name>\C$\Program Files\MATLAB".Start MATLAB using the newly mapped drive letter: Y:\R2016a\bin\matlab.exe instead of using the MATLAB icon or Start menu.If using NTFS, configure NTFS to enable short filename (SFN) support: Go to Windows Start menu and search for regedit.exe and run it.Navigate to the following location: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreationChange the value of NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation to 2 or 0. (Note: 2 is the default setting for Windows NTFS.)For more information on this registry setting, see: https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/cc778996(WS.10).aspx

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when using NaN with?the operators |, ||, &, &&
Description: In generated code, the operators??|,?||,?&,?&& ? do not error when passed a value containing a  NaN .? Instead the generated code treats the value  NaN  as true.? In MATLAB the operations result in an error.  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Description: When viewing custom documentation in the Help browser, the subsection headings in the left navigation pane do not expand to reveal sub-contents. This makes navigating the custom documentation difficult.

Summary: SDD report includes requirements only if top model has requirements
Description: The System Design Description (SDD) report should report all requirements linked to a model, regardless of whether the links occur in the model (i.e., the top model) or models that it references. However, the SDD fails to report requirements linked to model references if the top model is not linked to any requirements.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for a Variant Subsystem that is nested in control flow may be missing a required curly brace
Description: The generated code for a Variant Subsystem that is nested in control flow may be missing a required curly brace if the following are true: The generated code for the Variant Subsystem is in the false branch of a control flow construct. On the Code Generation>Comments  pane, the Simulink block/Stateflow object comments  parameter is not selected.  Without the curly brace, the generated code does not compile. For example, in the following generated code snippet, the code for the Variant Subsystem is in the else  statement, and the else  statement is false. As a result, at the beginning of the else  statement, the generated code is missing a required curly brace. if (x)  {...code...}  else  # if VARIANT  code for variant1  #else  code for variant2  #endif}
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you can try selecting the Simulink block/Stateflow object comments parameter.

Summary: Abs block might have incorrect coverage when the input and output of the Abs block have different data types
Description: The Abs block might report incorrect coverage when the input and output of the Abs block have different data types. For example: If the input to the Abs block is negative, it should satisfy the "U < 0" objective.??The coverage highlighting and report indicates that the "U < 0" objective was not satisfied even though it clearly was.

Summary: Resource Pool block does not work correctly with fractional amounts
Description: The Resource Acquire block acquires only integer amounts of resources, even if the corresponding Resource Pool block parameter Resource granularity  is set to Fractional amoun t.

Summary: nctinv can return inaccurate results for very large degrees of freedom
Description: One of the parameters of the noncentral t  distribution is its degrees of freedom, NU . Typically this parameter represents the effective number of observations used to estimate a variance. When this parameter gets very large, the results of the nctinv  (inverse cdf) function might become inaccurate. This might result in a warning message stating that the computations did not converge.
Workaround: As the degrees of freedom parameter NU gets large, the noncentral t distribution approaches the normal distribution, so for such values, instead of usingt = nctinv(P,NU,DELTA)you can uset = norminv(P,DELTA,1)

Summary: Updating Simscape model can hang or produce errors
Description: Updating some Simscape models can either hang or issue errors like: Expected scalar type. Got type array<logical, 1, 1>. This bug is triggered by Simscape equations containing: Exponents in arrays -- for example, [x, y^2]  Array exponents -- for example, x.^[1, 2]
Workaround: For equations containing exponents in arrays, use intermediate terms. For example, you can replace "[x, y^2]" with "[x, y_squared]", where y_squared is defined in a let clause as "y_squared = y^2;".For equations containing array exponents, convert "x.^[1, 2]" to "[x, x^2]", then use the first workaround.

Summary: Setting the Metadata parameter for an externally saved test harness results in an error when opening the test harness
Description: If you set the Metadata  parameter for an externally saved test harness, for example: set_param('Harness1,'Metadata',harnessMetadata)  Opening the test harness using the test harness badge or sltest.harness.open  can result in an error: Unable to load test harness 'Harness1'.  Caused by: The unique identifier for the test harness 'Harness1' saved in its SLX file does not match the identifier reserved for this harness. It is possible that this file is not correct for this harness.  Also, the test harness badge will not display the thumbnail image of the test harness.
Workaround: You can use the open_system command to open the test harness SLX file, open the harness file directly from the file pane, or follow the installation instructions below to open the test harness using the test harness badge or sltest.harness.open.Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB. For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.  To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                     Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems*:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  *Notes for Windows Systems:      You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode. Extract  the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulating cordic functions with fixed-point or integer input types can produce incorrect results when used with Xcode 7 through 7.2
Description: A bug in the clang compiler optimization in Xcode version 7.0 through 7.2 can cause code generated from cordic functions with fixed-point or integer input types to produce incorrect results. This issue occurs when you run a model in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode or use MEX functions in your model (e.g., a MATLAB Function block). It also occurs when you use fiaccel  or codegen  for generating MEX functions for your MATLAB code. The bug does not affect Linux or Windows platforms, nor Xcode versions older than 7.0.
Workaround: For Simulink, in the Configuration Parameters > All Parameters pane, set the Compiler optimization level parameter to Optimizations off (faster builds). Alternatively, from the command-line:>> set_param('model_name','SimCompilerOptimization','off')For MATLAB, turn off optimizations in the codegen and fiaccel commands using the -g option.You can also use Xcode version 6.4 or older to work around the issue.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when the original port of the Entity Replicator block becomes blocked
Description: When the Entity Replicate block Hold original entity until all replicas depart  check box is cleared and the block blocks an input port, it never later unblocks that input port, even after the original entity departs. This behavior causes incorrect simulation results.

Summary: Compilation failure for AUTOSAR generated code when Model block output connects directly to ExplicitSend root outport
Description: Generated code for an AUTOSAR model fails to compile under the following conditions: The model contains a referenced model. A referenced model output connects directly to a top model root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is ExplicitSend .  Under these conditions, the model build generates incorrect initialization function code, and subsequent compilation of the code fails.
Workaround: To work around this bug, add a Signal Conversion block between the output port of the referenced model and the output port of the top model. This forces creation of buffers that the explicit write can use in the initialization function.

Summary: Protecting a model with model reference variants fails
Description: For a model that uses model reference variants, model protection with code generation support fails with the following message: ?Could not find information file: /?  tmp/?  tp7bc37ca7_4a0b_4805_b878_44536dbb2f72/?  slprj/?  sim/?  rtwdemo_linl/?  tmwinternal/?  binfo_mdlref.mat.?? One reason this can happen is that the top model has the 'Rebuild options'? parameter set to 'Never' on the 'Model Referencing' pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog.
Workaround: To protect the model, you must disable model variants.

Summary: Justifications and comments are not saved automatically when closing Polyspace user interface
Description: In the 'Results Manager' perspective of the Polyspace user interface, you have to explicitly save your comments and justifications. Otherwise, when you close the Polyspace user interface, you lose the comments and justifications that you added on reviewed checks. No pop-up window is displayed.
Workaround: To make sure your comments and justifications are saved, before closing the user interface after each review session, select the menu item File > Save.

Summary: Using -output switch with generateAudioPlugin produces unloadable plug-in on MacOS X
Description: On MacOS X only, using the -output  switch of generateAudioPlugin  will produce a plug-in that cannot be loaded by a DAW. For example, the following command produces a plug-in named myPluginFileName.vst, but a DAW will not be able to load it. >> generateAudioPlugin -output myPluginFileName myPluginClassName
Workaround: Use the movefile command instead of the -output switch:>> generateAudioPlugin myPluginClassName>> movefile myPluginClassName.vst myPluginFileName.vst

Summary: ClassificationDiscriminant object might return incorrect predictions for quadratic discriminant types if you pass ClassNames to ClassificationDiscriminant.fit or fitcdiscr
Description: If you pass ClassNames  to ClassificationDiscriminant.fit  or fitcdiscr , and specify DiscrimType  as quadratic , pseudoQuadratic  or diagQuadratic , the returned ClassificationDiscriminant  object might be constructed incorrectly. The ClassificationDiscriminant  object might then predict incorrect class labels and class posterior probabilities. The object might also have incorrect values in the Coeffs  property.
Workaround: Use the default value for ClassNames, i.e., do not pass the ClassNames parameter to the ClassificationDiscriminant.fit method or fitcdiscr function for quadratic discriminant types

Summary: Missing data on Simulink Scope if the display is stretched across monitors
Description: If a Simulink Scope is stretched across multiple monitors, complete simulation data might not be visible on the Scope display.
Workaround: Increase the buffer size that holds Scope trace data by making changes to the MATLAB code.  In the MATLAB Command Window, entercd(matlabroot); cd toolbox\shared\spcuilib\+matlabshared\+scopes\+visual    Open the file TimeDomainSLADisplay.m in the MATLAB editor.     In functions createLine and createLineMagPhase,change hLine = hg2sample.ScopeLineAnimator('maxNumVertices',20000) to hLine = hg2sample.ScopeLineAnimator('maxNumVertices',25000) and change hLine = hg2sample.ScopeStairAnimator('maxNumVertices',20000) to hLine = hg2sample.ScopeStairAnimator('maxNumVertices',25000)    Save the file and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Imaginary function in a MATLAB function inside a Stateflow chart produces only real?result in code generation
Description: When?a Stateflow chart uses MATLAB as the action language and a function is specified to be real, the result does not produce an error.?Instead, the result ignores the imaginary part and produces the real part. Given a MATLAB function foo(a)  where the input a  is set to be real and a call to foo  from inside a MATLAB function such as: foo(5+3i); ? The expected behavior for this function is to cause an error,?because the complex value 5+3i cannot be fed to a function foo() ,  whose input is real. Instead, code generation or simulation proceeds and foo()  is called with the input value 5 . As a result, the imaginary part of the input is discarded.? This behavior occurs only if the call to foo()  is inside a MATLAB function that is inside a Stateflow chart.?
Workaround: Do not pass nonreal inputs to functions that require real inputs, or explicitly discard the imaginary part first.

Summary: Single project definition file preference is ignored when creating new projects
Description: If your Simulink Project preference for project definition files is set to a single file, this setting is ignored when you create a new project using the start page in R2016a. You get a project using the default multiple project definition files. Multiple definition files are better for avoiding merge issues on shared projects. A single definition file is not recommended for source control, because this option is likely to cause merge issues when multiple users submit changes in the same project.
Workaround: To change the project definition file type, use the?export?function.To create a project from the exported archive, see?Create a New Project from an Archived Project.

Summary: Simulink.Bus.CreateMATLABStruct can error when passed port handles and model uses a configuration set reference
Description: When both  these conditions are true, using Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruc t throws an error:  The function is passed a port handle or an array of port handles from bus signal ports in a model.  That model uses a configuration set reference, rather than a local configuration set.  The following error is thrown:  Error using Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>switchFlagsBeforeCompile (line 483) A configuration set reference does not allow writing to parameters in the source configuration set Error in Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>compileModelAndGetStruct (line 423) pFlags = switchFlagsBeforeCompile(model, pFlags); Error in Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>createMATLABStructFromPortHandle (line 399) mStruct = compileModelAndGetStruct(model, portHandles, false, alreadyCompiled, false); Error in Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct (line 203) mStruct = createMATLABStructFromPortHandle(obj, pStruct);
Workaround: To work around this issue, attach a temporary local configuration set to the model and set it to active. After this, Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct should work as expected. Once the use of the function is complete, you can remove the local configuration set from the model.

Summary: scatterm function errors when color data input is m-by-3 and at least one point falls outside map limits
Description: (a) If you specify the fourth input to the scatterm  function as an m -by-3 RGB color array and  one or more input points fall outside the limits of the map, scatterm  issues an error. The error implies, erroneously, that the input color data is invalid. (b) In addition, in the special case in which the latitude and longitude input vectors have exactly 3 elements and  a uniform color is requested via a color specification, scatterm  fails to display some points, and displays others with the wrong color.?
Workaround: In case (a), if you have a map with quadrangular map limits (which includes conic, cylindrical, and pseudo-cylindrical projections in their normal aspects), you can use the ingeoquad function to pre-filter data outside the map's bounding quadrangle before calling scatterm. In case (b), you can replicate the coordinates (and size value, if non-scalar) of one of the points, so the latitude and longitude vectors have 4 elements instead of 3.

Summary: Simulink Real-Time target computer executing PTP fails to act as slave node?to 3rd party PTP master node
Description: You want to use the IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol (PTP) ?to synchronize the clocks of two applications. One is running a Simulink Real-Time application; the other is running an application on a?third-party hardware and software system. You want the Simulink Real-Time application to serve as PTP slave node and the other application to serve as PTP master node. When the two nodes are connected and started, the Simulink Real-Time node enters the Slave state but does not synchronize its clock to the master clock. Therefore, the two nodes fail to synchronize their PTP clocks.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.?Change log:?? ? ? ?September 23, 2016: attachment_1357362_15b)16a_win32_win64_2016-09-23.zip: initial version, works on R2015b and R2016a only.

Summary: During code generation for ERT targets, name collision between a Simulink Function Caller block function call and subsystem block name causes access violations and MATLAB crashes.
Description: When generating code for an ERT or ERT derived target and the model includes a Simulink Function Caller block that calls a function with the same name as a subsystem block in the model, MATLAB crashes due to a memory access violation.
Workaround: Check the model to make sure that it does not include a subsystem block using the same name as a function that is called by a Simulink Function Caller block.

Summary: Generating code for models with 'Optimize using the specified minimum and maximum values' optimization enabled crashes MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash when generating code for models if all  of these conditions are true: The model has Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Optimize using the specified minimum and maximum values  parameter enabled. The model has a atomic subsystem with the Function packaging  parameter for the subsystem set to Reusable function . One or more inputs to the subsystem originates from a nonvirtual bus source.
Workaround: Clear the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Optimize using the specified minimum and maximum values parameter.

Summary: Commenting out a Floating Scope block causes a MATLAB crash
Description: If you comment out a Floating Scope block in Simulink, a MATLAB crash will result when attempting to open the scope or run the model.
Workaround: Remove the Floating Scope block instead of commenting out.Detach all the signals from the Floating Scope.

Summary: Incorrect linearization results for Rate Transition block when using fixed-step solver
Description: Linearizing Simulink models that contain Rate Transition blocks might produce incorrect results when the model uses a fixed-step solver. If a Rate Transition block in the linearization path of such a model acts as a unit delay, the block might not linearize correctly. When working as a unit delay, the Rate Transition block has an output sample time that is different from its input sample time. This difference causes a linearization error when using the linearize  command or the Linear Analysis Tool.

Description: For Scope blocks connected to a mix of Stateflow state signals and signals of other data types such as doubles, you can encounter this bug in this situation: Run the Simulink model with Stateflow charts and see state activity in a Scope block. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation and code generation mismatch when atomic subsystem with periodic sample time contains Constant blocks
Description: Consider an atomic subsystem with a periodic discrete sample time. If this subsystem contains a Constant block that feeds into a For Iterator Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or If Action Subsystem, the results you see during simulation and code generation might be different.
Workaround: Change the sample time specification of Constant blocks in atomic subsystems with periodic sample times. Open the Block Parameters dialog box of the Constant block and specify Sample time as -1.

Summary: Running a model from a working directory with an existing slprj folder can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB can crash if there is an existing slprj  folder in your working directory when you run your model containing Stateflow blocks or MATLAB Function blocks. The crash occurs when there is an issue writing to the slprj  folder (for example, you do not have write access permission).
Workaround: To reduce the possibility of crashing, run the attached p-file when you start your MATLAB session. However, if you do not have write permission to the slprj folder, you will still get an error message when you try to run the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for MATLAB function block or Stateflow Chart?containing variable-sized arrays may be incorrect
Description: For a model that meets either of the following conditions, the generated code may contain incorrectly-sized output array:  The model contains a MATLAB function block with a variable size output. The model contains a Stateflow Chart with a variable size local or chart output.  The MATLAB function or Stateflow Chart changes the size of the variable by doing one of the following: A null assignment operation like var(rows,:) = [];  A user-defined function with reusable in/out arguments with?the same name.  The incorrect generated code has a temporary size array that is a copy of the variable. This copy is passed to a function call along with the original data array. ?The function updates the temporary size array. ?The following is a sample of the incorrect generated code: tmp_sizes[0] = matrix_size[0];tmp_sizes[1] = matrix_size[1];functionCall(matrix_data, tmp_sizes);
Workaround: Change the name of either the input argument or output argument of the MATLAB function, so that the names are no longer the same and avoid using the null assignment on the chart variables or?MATLAB function output.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Cosine and sine of large, pure imaginary numbers produce NaNs
Description: In generated code, cosine and sine of large, pure imaginary numbers produce complex results with NaNs.? For example, cos(1000*i)  returns Inf + NaN*i  instead of ?Inf + 0*i , and sin(1000*i)  returns NaN + Inf*i? instead of 0?+ Inf*i .

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may produce misleading results when analyzing a model having variants
Description: Simulink Design Verifier supports analysis of model having variants by analyzing only the active variant of the model. Simulink Design Verifier may produce misleading results when:The model has multiple variants controlled by the variant parameters Simulink Design Verifier is configured to analyze value ranges of variant parameters  In these cases, Simulink Design Verifier only analyzes the active variant of the model, but shows the result which indicates that all specified value ranges of variant parameters have been analyzed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant Model block propagating conditions to top model may show incorrect values
Description: A Variant Model block can refer to multiple Model blocks. If any of these Variant Model?blocks are propagating condition out of the model, it may cause?incorrect values.?  Note: From R2019a,?Variant Model blocks are no longer supported.?Instead, use Variant Subsystem blocks with Model blocks as choices.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder generates incorrect code for dimension variants with division operation
Description: Embedded Coder generates incorrect code for dimension variants with division operations, if those divide a term that contains a plus or minus operation. For example, a dimension variant with the expression (A+B)/C  is incorrectly generated as A/C+B/C .
Workaround: Avoid division operations in dimension variants.

Description: If a user tries to train a network where the image input layer uses the data augmentations 'randcrop' and 'randfliplr' and the normalization 'zerocenter', an error will be thrown when the user calls 'trainNetwork'.

Summary: False green check on a pointer to a shared variable
Description: Suppose a multitasking program has a pointer ptr  that points to a shared variable x  but is itself not shared. Even though ptr  is not shared, *ptr  can be modified by multiple tasks because x  is shared between multiple tasks. In some cases (with some specific coding patterns), the verification does not take this fact into account. For instance, in the example below, *ptr  can have both values 2 and 22, because x  can have both values. However, the verification does not take this into account. Therefore, some of the checks are incorrect. /* Use option -entry-points T1,T2 */  volatile int rd;int x;int y = 128;int * ptr = &x;void T1(void) { x = 22; }void T2(void) {int tmp;*ptr = 2;assert(x==2 || x==22); /* assert check correctly green, x can be 2 or 22 */if (rd) assert((*ptr == 2)); /* assert check green, should be orange */if (rd) assert((*ptr == 22)); /* assert check red, should be orange */tmp = *ptr;if (rd) assert(tmp==2 || tmp==22); /* Incorrect unreachable check on tmp==22 */ptr = &y;*ptr = 256;}  int main(void) {}

Summary: Accessing structure element offsets using C API might result in wrong values
Description: For structure data using C API , the element offset is stored as a 16-bit unsigned integer. Hence, offsets larger than 2^16 bits might result in wrong answers due to overflow. Rapid accelerator mode leverages C API to tune structure parameters. Hence, using tunable structure parameters whose element offset size exceeds 2^16 bits will silently produce wrong answers when simulated in rapid accelerator mode.

Summary: Loading a DOM object does not restore its Parent and Children properties
Description: Loading a DOM object does not restore its Parent and Children properties. For example, running this script results in a text object with an empty Parent property and a paragraph object with an empty Children property: import mlreportgen.dom.* t = Text('Hello World')p = Paragraph(t)save('dom','p','t') clear('p','t'); load('dom');

Summary: MATLAB crashes during fast restart or single-stepping of an incomplete SimEvents model
Description: A SimEvents model that does not generate any entities using an Entity Generator or Message Send block can cause MATLAB to crash when using fast restart or simulation stepping with the Simulation Stepper.
Workaround: As a workaround, add an Entity Generator block or a Message Send block to your model.

Summary: MATLAB might crash with an unhandled C++ exception when a file named services.dll is on the system path
Description: If there is a file named services.dll  on the system path, MATLAB attempts to load this file during startup even if it is not associated with MATLAB. This might cause MATLAB to crash with an unhandled C++ exception.
Workaround: You need write permission on the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/matlab.settings XML file.Edit the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/matlab.settings XML file and replace packagename="services" with packagename="libmwservices".?

Summary: MEX builds might not use compiler flags added?with?coder.updateBuildInfo
Description: If you specify?compiler flags by using??coder.updateBuildInfo('addCompileFlags',...) , for a MEX build, MATLAB Coder might not pass the compiler flags to the C/C++ compiler.
Workaround: Add the flags to the group 'OPTS'?so that the MEX build uses them. For example, the following code?adds the flag '-customOption'?to the beginning of the C or C++ compiler flags.function y = ex(x)coder.updateBuildInfo('addCompileFlags','-customOption','OPTS');y = x;

Summary: AUTOSAR model validation fails to find variable defined in data dictionary
Description: The autosar.api.validateModel  function can generate an error if both of these conditions are true:  The target model (myModel ) references another model (mySubModel ) through a Model block. mySubModel  is linked to a Simulink data dictionary (mySubDict ).   The error indicates that the function cannot find variables that are defined in mySubDict .

Summary: MATLAB opens incorrect file if an error occurs while stopped at an error breakpoint
Description: If you execute a command in the Command Window that causes an error while MATLAB is already stopped at an error breakpoint (created using the dbstop if error  command), MATLAB opens the wrong file. Instead of opening the file where the new error occured, MATLAB opens the first where the first error occured.
Workaround: To ensure MATLAB opens the correct file, run the command that resulted in the second error from within a script, instead of in the Command Window.

Summary: The PowerPoint API can generate files that PowerPoint cannot open
Description: The PowerPoint API generates an output file of the same format (e.g., presentation) as its input, regardless of the file extensions you specify. As a result, you can inadvertently generate a file whose format (e.g., template) does not match its extension (e.g., pptx). If you attempt to open such a file in PowerPoint, PowerPoint displays a file format and extension mismatch error. For example, the following script generates a file that PowerPoint cannot open because its format (template) does not match its extension.  import mlreportgen.ppt.*% mytemplate.potx is a valid PowerPoint template% mypres.pptx will be a template, despite the extensionslides = Presentation('mypres.pptx', 'mytemplate.potx');slide1 = add(slides,'Title Slide');replace(slide1,'Title','My Presentation');replace(slide1,'Subtitle','Create a Presentation Program');close(slides);if ispc    % PowerPoint will think that myppt is a presentation because    % of the extension. Actually, it is a template. This causes    % PowerPoint to display a file type error.    winopen('myppt.pptx');end ? ? ? ? ?
Workaround: To generate a presentation, use presentations (.pptx files). To generate templates, use templates (.potx files).

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when using MATLAB Function block with Continuous sample time and persistent variables
Description: If you configure a MATLAB Function block to have a Continuous sample time and it uses persistent variables, Simulink can produce incorrect results if a zero-crossing is detected by the solver during simulation.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can disable JIT for the MATLAB Function block by doing either of the following:Set a breakpoint somewhere in the MATLAB Function blockRun the attached p-file during your MATLAB session before running the model. This will disable JIT for all MATLAB Function blocks and Stateflow charts for the session.

Summary: Incorrect value for number of Header Files code metric
Description: When using option Calculate code metrics  (-code-metrics ), the number of Header Files  counted can be incorrect. Consider the following code: /* Hello World */#include <assert.h>#include "misra.h"int main(void){  assert(1);   printf("Hello World\n");  return 0;}  For this code, Polyspace counts 1  header file. Only one of the two included files is counted because Polyspace only counts application header files and not system header files. Polyspace should count application header files and the generic Polyspace headers used to compile the source code. The header files required to compile this code example are: matlabroot\polyspace\verifier\cxx\cinclude\__polyspace__std_decls.h  matlabroot\polyspace\verifier\cxx\cinclude\assert.h  matlabroot\polyspace\verifier\cxx\cinclude\stdarg.h  matlabroot\polyspace\verifier\cxx\cinclude\__polyspace__libc_defs.h  mysourcepathto\misra.h   So the Header Files  code metric value should be 5.
Workaround: Please install worarkound associated with https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/1396653

Summary: The Video Viewer block does not display an imported image when running a simulation.
Description: When you set sample time to inf  in the Image From File  block with it connected to the  Video Viewer  block, the simulation cannot display an imported image.
Workaround: Follow one of the two workarounds: Change the sample time for the image source block to a sample period other than inf. Use the To Video Display block instead of the Video Viewer block.

Summary: Opening a model or subsystem with a very large, but mostly empty, canvas can cause Simulink to use more memory than necessary, perhaps leading to an out-of-memory situation
Description: Simulink creates canvases (offscreen image buffers) for the block diagrams that are opened, based on the sizes (extents) specified in the .slx  or .mdl  file. If  these extents are much larger than normal, the amount of memory used can be greater than normal. Possible ways this situation can occur include: The model was auto-generated. The model was manually modified.
Workaround: Fit the system to fit the window. From within an open model or subsystem, click the space bar. Fitting the system to the window resets the extents to the minimum size, while still maintaining a view of the entire system, which in turn reduces the ammount of memory that Simulink uses.

Description: Building a model fails when using a system target derived from a TLC target file.  The build error reports that a parameter cannot be found.

Summary: verLessThan returns incorrect result when queried with release string instead of version number
Description: verLessThan  always returns false  when queried with a release string, even when called from a version of MATLAB? that is older than the version specified by the release string. For example, when executed on a R2014b installation, verLessThan  in the following code always returns false  (for which the expected answer should be true ). if verLessThan('matlab', 'R2015b') % Version 8.5     % code intended to run on R2015a and earlierelse    % code intended to run on R2015b and laterend As a result, code intended only for releases R2015b or above runs in an older release. ?
Workaround: Use version numbers to query verLessThan instead of release strings:if verLessThan('matlab', '8.5')    % codes intended to run on R2015a and earlierelse    % codes intended to run on R2015b and laterend

Summary: Crash when using parfor, spmd, anonymous functions, and return in one function
Description: MATLAB might crash if you use parfor , spmd , anonymous functions, and return  in one function. For example, MATLAB crashes if you execute the following function: function myFunction   f = @()1;   return;   spmd       z = rand(2);   end   parfor i=1:1       y(i) = 1;   endend
Workaround: Use named local functions instead of anonymous functions. For example:function myFunction   f = myLocalFunction;   return;   spmd       z = rand(2);   end   parfor i=1:1       y(i) = 1;   endendfunction f = myLocalFunction   f = 1;end

Summary: In weboptions, ArrayFormat values 'php' and 'json' fail
Description: When using webread , webwrite  or websave  with weboptions , the ArrayFormat  values of 'php'  and 'json'  fail with an error such as: Error using webread (line 119)Invalid URL: '"http://www.server.com/?foo=[1,2,3]" (check for unencoded or \ characters in URL in URL)'.
Workaround: For the 'json' option, manually create the query value from the array. For example, the commands: a = [1 2 3]; webread('http://server.com','foo',['[' strjoin(cellstr(num2str(a(:))),',') ']'])send the URL: http://server.com?foo=[1,2,3]For the 'php' option, use the 'repeating' format and rename the QueryName argument to contain '[]' in its name. For example, the command: webread('http://server.com', 'foo[]', [1 2 3], weboptions('ArrayFormat','repeating'))sends the URL: http://server.com?foo[]=1&foo[]=2&foo[]=3

Summary: Code generation with dynamic memory allocation omits variant conditionals from generated C++ class constructor/destructor.
Description: The code generator omits variant condition guards within a generated C++ class constructor/destructor when: The model contains a variant model reference block. The model configuration sets the 'Code interface packaging'  parameter value to 'C++ class' . The model configuration sets the 'Use dynamic memory allocation for model block instantiation'  parameter value to 'on' .  ?
Workaround: To produce variant conditionals in the generated C++ class constructor/destructor, set the model 'Use dynamic memory allocation for model block instantiation' parameter value to 'off'.

Summary: Generating code for a model that contains a Ground Source block inside a Simulink Function may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB may crash during code generation for a model that meeets the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow Chart that calls a Simulink Function. The Simulink Function contains a Ground Source block that connects to a Bus Creator block.

Summary: Compatibility check for Stateflow model fails when analysis mode is changed
Description: For a compatible model containing Stateflow elements, the model becomes incompatible when the mode of analysis is changed between two runs, such as from "Test Generation" to "Property Proving".
Workaround: Delete the /slprj folder and rerun the check.

Summary: Serial blocks send and receive incorrect big-endian data
Description: If you send or receive big-endian data with Serial Send or Serial Receive blocks, the endianess setting does not take effect. Data is still sent and received in little-endian.

Summary: Scatter plot data points with NaN CData are visible but should be invisible
Description: Scatter plot data points that correspond to NaN  CData  values do not render parts of the marker as transparent when either the?MarkerFaceColor  or MarkerEdgeColor  is set to 'flat' .
Workaround: If both MarkerFaceColor and MarkerEdgeColor are set to 'flat', 'none', or one of each, remove the data points that correspond to NaN values. For example:x = 1:4;y = 1:4;a = [36 49 36 49];c = [1 NaN NaN 1];s = scatter(x,y,a,c);keepPoints = ~isnan(s.CData);s = scatter(x(keepPoints), y(keepPoints), ...    a(keepPoints), c(keepPoints));If either MarkerFaceColor or MarkerEdgeColor is set to 'flat', and the other is set to an RGB triplet, create two scatter plots in the same axes. First, plot the points that are associated with the NaN values. Set the appropriate property (MarkerFaceColor or MarkerEdgeColor) to 'none'. In the second plot, plot the points that are not associated with NaN values. For example:x = 1:4;y = 1:4;a = [36 49 36 49];c = [1 NaN NaN 1];s = scatter(x,y,a,c,'filled','MarkerEdgeColor','k');% Plot NaN points with unfilled markersnanPoints = isnan(s.CData);nonNaNPoints = ~nanPoints;sNaN = scatter(x(nanPoints), y(nanPoints), ...    a(nanPoints), c(nanPoints),'k');hold on% Plot Non-NaN points with filled markerssNonNaN = scatter(x(nonNaNPoints), y(nonNaNPoints), ...    a(nonNaNPoints), c(nonNaNPoints), 'filled','MarkerEdgeColor','k');hold off

Summary: Calling intlinprog repeatedly causes?MATLAB to use more?memory
Description: If you call intlinprog  repeatedly, in a loop or some other way, MATLAB uses more memory at each call. Eventually, this memory leak can make MATLAB run out of memory entirely and halt or error.

Summary: polyspace-configure in Cygwin consumes memory
Description: When you launch polyspace-configure  and trace your build command in a Cygwin environment, polyspace-configure  consumes too much memory and does not exit.
Workaround: Installation instructions :Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwritefiles in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation. By default this is in C:\ProgramFiles\MATLAB\<version> under Windows. This folder is called <MATLAB>in subsequent steps.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Reportand store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file(open the .zip file and look at the contents).Extract the files from the .zip file.Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>folder.Check that binaries have appropriate 'read & execute'permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>folder.Notes for Windows Systems:If you see "Error: Access is denied" when attempting to extractthe .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Dothis by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry,right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Truth Table blocks might give wrong output when condition label is identical to a data name
Description: In a Truth Table block, when a data name that you defined is identical to a condition label name, the truth table might have incorrect output. This bug occurs in Truth Table blocks but not truth table functions in a chart.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code for a model with floating point to fixed point conversions may result in incorrect generated code
Description: During the code generation process, Simulink may ignore the model setting Use floating-point multiplication to handle net slope corrections  when performing internal calculations. This parameter can be found in the Configuration Parameters > Optimization  pane.  If the bug is triggered, values in the generated code will differ from simulation values. This difference is the result of using floating-point division versus floating-point multiplication of a reciprocal. The following conditions must be true to trigger this bug: The Use floating-point multiplication to handle net slope corrections  parameter is set to On.  The model contains conversions from constant floating-point values to fixed-point types that have non-power of two slopes.
Workaround: Set the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Use floating-point multiplication to handle net slope corrections parameter to Off.Alternatively, at the command-line enter:set_param(Model_name, 'UseFloatMulNetSlope', 'Off')This workaround will impact all conversions from floating point to fixed point.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when dragging blocks from a library into Simulink Editor
Description: When adding?some blocks to a model by dragging it from the Library Browser to the Simulink Editor, MATLAB can crash. The crash can occur with masked subsystems and might occur with other library blocks.? Adding blocks using?the quick insert menu on the model canvas or add_block  does not cause a crash.

Description: On a 5K resolution retina iMac, Video Viewer does not display any video in certain cases. This issue is seen with both the implay  function and the Video Viewer blocks for videos with specific resolutions.
