Summary: Error using When decomposition with non-unique step names in a Test Sequence or Test Assessment block
Description: Using a When  decomposition in a Test Sequence block with substeps that have non-unique step names can result in the following error. This includes steps that have the same name as other steps in other hierarchy levels. Symbol 'step_1' could resolve to the state 'step_1', or to the state 'step_1' of the same name. Please rename one of these objects.
Workaround: Rename steps to use unique names from all other steps in the test sequence.

Summary: Buffer overflow vulnerability (CWE-120) in the FlexNet License Manager used by MathWorks software.
Description: Flexera Software has disclosed a buffer overflow vulnerability (CWE-120)  in the FlexNet License Manager, which is used for licensing MathWorks software. This License manager is vulnerable to buffer overflow attacks.  Buffer overflow condition exists when a program attempts to put more data than it can hold, and overwrite the memory outside the boundaries of the buffer. The security vulnerability is discovered in 'Lmgrd executable' and 'vendor daemon (MLM)' components of FlexNet Publisher. This was released under CVE number CVE-2015-8277 . This only affects the License Server, not MATLAB.
Workaround: MathWorks has incorporated a software update from Flexera. It can be downloaded from MathWorks License Manager download page. The patched version is FlexNet version 11.13.1.2.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink computes incorrect bounds for variable size signal and concat operation from MATLAB Function block
Description: Simulink tries to compute the size of a variable-size matrix in a concat  operation.? This produces incorrect code if a variable size matrix is concatenated with a static size matrix. An example of? MATLAB code in a MATLAB Function Block that induces this error is:   function P2   = fut_ans_mismatch  coder.varsize('P2', [35 30], [1 1]);  P1 = randi([0 0]);  P2 = [ones([12 20 1 1]); ones([12 20 1 1]); ones([P1 20 1 1]); ];

Summary: Model fails to update because Simulink cannot derive input port dimensions of a Merge block from its output port dimensions.
Description: Your model can have this problem when it contains a Merge block with all of the following conditions:  Signal dimensions have not been specified for any of its input signals. For example, all inputs are grounded or connected indirectly to root Inport block whose Dimensions property is specified as -1. Signal dimension has been specified for its output signal. For example, the output signal is connected directly to an Outport block with Dimensions property explicitly specified (not equal to -1).  The Merge block lacks the logic to back propagate output signal dimension to its input signals. Consequently, the input port dimensions are left unspecified until Simulink applies heuristics to derive the input signal dimensions. Such heuristics may result in input dimensions that are not consistent with the previously specified output dimensions, resulting in an error during block diagram update.
Workaround: Move the dimension specification from the output signal of the Merge block to one of its input signals. Otherwise, you must ?use Signal Specification blocks to specify dimensions for all input signals to match the expected output port dimensions.?

Summary: intlinprog?loses linear constraint feasibility while using a nondefault integer tolerance.
Description: An  intlinprog  solution can fail to satisfy linear constraints to within tolerances?when the IntegerTolerance  option is set to a value larger than its default. intlinprog  reports this infeasibility when it halts as error code -2_4  and?states: INTLINPROG encountered an internal error that caused the solution to lose integer and/  or linear constraint feasibility

Summary: Additional C files compiled with model reference SIM target do not have MATLAB_MEX_FILE defined
Description: If additional C source code is compiled with the model reference SIM target, via a mechanism such as rtwmakecfg.m , then the symbol MATLAB_MEX_FILE  is not defined when compiling that code. This behavior is inconsistent with Simulink Accelerator.
Workaround: To ensure that code runs for model reference SIM target, you can use the additional define MDL_REF_SIM_TGT. So to protect code to only run for simulation targets replace,#ifdef MATLAB_MEX_FILE/* code for simulation */#endifwith this code,#if defined(MATLAB_MEX_FILE) || defined(MDL_REF_SIM_TGT)/* code for simulation */#endif

Summary: Fixed-point conversion with dynamic memory allocation disabled can cause code generation to fail
Description: If you disable dynamic memory allocation, and then convert your MATLAB code to fixed-point, code generation can fail because the code generator might not find upper bounds for your data. Fixed-point conversion introduces fixed-point values for size arguments. For example: zeros(fi(x,1,32,0)) Upper bounds analysis does not support these fixed-point values.  ?
Workaround: To specify the type for a size argument, use a  built-in integer type instead of a fi object. For example, replace zeros(fi(x,1,32,0)) with zeros(int32(x)).

Summary: Embedded Coder can generate the same local variable inside and outside the local scope of Variant Subsystem
Description: For a Variant Subsystem with preprocessor conditionals, the generated code may define the same local variable both inside and outside the local scope of the Variant Subsystem. The following code snippet provides an example: void model_step() {?  int my_local_i32;?  ...?  #if VARIANT ?   {?  ? ? int my_local_i32;  ? ? ...?  }?  }?  ?

Summary: sldvlogsignals?errors out with Invalid value for 'Model Block' parameter
Description: If a block inherits [Inf, Inf] sample time, even when all user-given values are discrete, sldvlogsignals  gives the following error: ?Invalid value for 'Model block' parameter. The Model block specified by this parameter must reference a model having discrete sample time with zero offset.

Summary: linprog returns the error "Index exceeds matrix dimensions"
Description: The linprog  'interior-point'  algorithm (R2015a and prior) and linprog 'interior-point-legacy'  algorithm (R2015b and later) can return the error "Index exceeds matrix dimension " when computing the Lagrange multipliers lambda  or the output  argument. This error occurs for problems where both: Some decision variables are unbounded below and are bounded above, and The linear inequality matrix has linearly-dependent rows or rows with only one nonzero element.
Workaround: There are several potential workarounds:1. In R2015b and later, use the 'interior-point' algorithm:[x,fval,exitflag,output,lambda] = linprog(f,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,x0,optimoptions('linprog','algorithm','interior-point'));2. In R2010b and later, request at most three output arguments: [x,fval,exitflag].3. In R2010a and prior, request at most four output arguments [x,fval,exitflag,output].4. Manually identify and remove linearly dependent rows from the inequality matrix A and corresponding right-hand side b. Move inequalities with only one non-zero element to lower or upper bounds.5. Solve an equivalent problem where no decision varriables are both unbounded below and bounded above, by reflecting these variables:% Identify the indices of the variables that are unbounded below and bounded aboveidx = ~isfinite(lb) & isfinite(ub);% Reflect the corresponding coefficients in the objectivefr = f;fr(idx) = -fr(idx);% Reflect the corresponding coefficients in the inequalitiesAr = A;br = b;Ar(:,idx) = -Ar(:,idx);% Reflect the corresponding coefficients in the equalitiesAeqr = Aeq;beqr = beq;Aeqr(:,idx) = -Aeqr(:,idx);% Reflect the corresponding lower and upper boundslbr = lb;ubr = ub;lbr(idx) = -ub(idx);ubr(idx) = -lb(idx);% Reflect the corresponding initial pointsx0r = x0;x0r(idx) = -x0(idx);% Solve the reflected problem[xr,fvalr,exitflagr,outputr,lambdar] = linprog(fr,Ar,br,Aeqr,beqr,lbr,ubr,x0r);% To obtain the solution of the initial problem, reflect the corresponding% indices in the solutionx = xr;x(idx) = -x(idx);6. Solve an equivalent problem with lower bounds for all decision variables. To create the problem easily, first solve the problem by requesting only one output argument. Then use the solution to determine finite lower bounds, and re-solve the problem with the desired output arguments:% Solve the problem a first timex1 = linprog(f,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,x0);% Use the solution to determine plausible lower bounds, such aslb2 = min(x1,ub) - 10;% Solve the problem a second time with the desired outputs[x2,fval2,exitflag2,output2,lambda2] = linprog(f,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb2,ub,x0);Note: If the problem has dependent inequality constraints, there may exist multiple equivalent solutions, so techniques 5 and 6 may return different solutions. Solution 6 requires solving the problem twice and may not be appropriate for time-consuming problems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts with MATLAB variable and inline variant blocks may cause incorrect results?
Description: Simulink model may generate incorrect results when model contains below cases  Two or more Stateflow charts interfacing with each other using MATLAB variable? Any of the above Stateflow charts having condition from the Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks has Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  selected

Summary: The coverage report can differ if the model is executed with the command line (using sim or cvsim) or by using the play button on the UI and the model has Vector Concatenation block
Description: The Vector Concatenation block reports 0% execution coverage if the model is executed from the command line for semi-virtual concatenation. Block is semi-virtual (has no OutputFcn) when inputs map to contiguous memory chunks, such as for Vector Concatenation or Horizontal Matrix Concatenation blocks.
Workaround: Turn off execution coverage metrics in the coverage report, using Coverage Settings>Reporting>Settings>Filter Execution metric from report.

Summary: Cannot rename or move folders named in AdditionalPaths for a job on Windows
Description: If you add a folder to the AdditionalPaths property of a job submitted to the MATLAB Job Scheduler, then its parent folder remains locked by the worker after the job has finished. This means that you cannot rename or move the parent folder. Example: >> c = parcluster('MyMJS'); >> j = batch(c, @rand, 1, 'AdditionalPaths', 'C:\some\folder'); >> wait(j) You cannot rename the folder 'C:\some'. An error dialog is shown that states: "The action can't be completed because the folder or a file in it is open in another program"
Workaround: This can be prevented by adding both a taskStartup.m and a taskFinish.m. These scripts add and remove the additional paths at the start and end of every task respectively.1. Create a taskStartup.m file with the following contents:function taskStartup(task)addpath(task.Parent.AdditionalPaths{:})2. Create a taskFinish.m file with the following contents:function taskFinish(task)rmpath(task.Parent.AdditionalPaths{:})3. Follow the instructions in the documentation to add these functions to the job:taskStartup documentationtaskFinish documentation

Summary: Portable code for SIL and PIL is not generated when test hardware and production hardware settings match

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for loops that access the same matrix sections
Description: Incorrectly generated code might occur for MATLAB code that contains loops that modify the same sections of a matrix using different index accesses. This bug might also occur for Simulink code generation of a model that results in the generated C code below. For example, consider the following MATLAB code that contains a copy to matrix A  from matrix B  and C  within a for  loop as follows: function A = memcpy_example_1318579(A,B,C)  ? ? for i=1:10  ? ? ? ? A(i) = B(i);  ? ? ? ? A(i+5) = C(i);  ? ? end  end?  There is an overlap in the access of A[6...10] . The incorrectly generated code contains two distinct memcpy functions of the following form:? ? memcpy(&A[0], &B[0], 10U * sizeof(double));  ?memcpy(&A[5], &C[0], 10U * sizeof(double));  The correctly generated code is as follows:? ? for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {  ? ? A[i] = B[i];  ? ? A[i + 5] = C[i];  ? }  To reproduce this issue, run 'run_memcpy_example_1318579.m'  file in the attached examples in? 'example_1318579.zip' .
Workaround: Set?Use memcpy for vector assignment?to?Off, or, set the?Memcpy threshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?int32(intmax).

Summary: Simulink Coder may not warn that an Enum value is larger than the target's C signed integer
Description: Model simulation and code generation may fail for a Stateflow Chart that meets the following conditions: The Action Language  parameter is set to C . Expressions contain values of an integer-based enumerated data type that is of a Simulink.IntEnumType  class definition. At least one of the values of the enumerated data type is larger than the target's C  signed integer. You can find the number of bits for your target's signed integer on the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box. The Stateflow Chart does not contain any data of the Enum: <class name>  type.  When the failure occurs, the error message is similar to the following:Unable to create mex function model.mexa64 required for simulation. The error message does not state that an Enum  value is larger than the target's C signed integer.
Workaround: Add data to your chart of the type Enum: <class name>. Assign enumerated values to this data.

Summary: AUTOSAR createComponentAsModel generates error if ARXML code?uses long identifiers
Description: The AUTOSAR importer function createComponentAsModel  creates a model using information from ARXML files.? If the ARXML code contains long identifiers, createComponentAsModel  might fail with the error below. Error using autosar.mm.mm2sl.MRLayoutManager/   addGotoFrom (line 265)?    The value 'very_long_identifier_that_exceeds_limits_of_importing_into_Simulink' of parameter ? 'GotoTag' is not a valid MATLAB identifier. Valid identifiers start with an alphabetic ? character, followed by at most 63 alphanumeric or '_' characters ?
Workaround: Option 1:?Work Around?the IssueUse ARXML identifiers less than 55 characters long.Option 2: Apply a Patch to Fix the IssueThe following steps apply only to Embedded Coder Support Package for AUTOSAR Standard version 15.2.6. Check your installed support package version using the command?matlabshared.supportpkg.getInstalled.Download and install?attachment_1315346_autosar_spkg_15_2_6_2016-08-24.zip.Installation Instructions for AUTOSAR Support PackageLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your AUTOSAR installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the?root?account.Locate your AUTOSAR installation by typing?autosar_get_root_directory?at the MATLAB command prompt. The path that precedes "toolbox" will be referred to as?<AUTOSAR>?in subsequent steps. For example, if?autosar_get_root_directory?returns "C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2015b\autosarstandard\toolbox\coder\autosar",?<AUTOSAR>?will be "C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2015b\autosarstandard".Exit MATLAB.Download the attached?.zip?file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the?<AUTOSAR>?folder. If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking?Open Safe Files After Downloading?in?Preferences > GeneralMake a backup copy of each of the files listed in the?.zip?file. To see the contents of the?.zip?file:Windows Systems*:?Open the?.zip?file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems:?Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <AUTOSAR>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the?.zip?file.Windows Systems*:Open the?.zip?file.Extract the contents of the file to your?<AUTOSAR>?folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems:?Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):?cd <AUTOSAR>unzip <.zip file>Delete the?.zip?file in the?<AUTOSAR>?folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the?.zip?file in the?<AUTOSAR>?folder. To save the?.zip?file in the?<AUTOSAR>?folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the?.zip?file(s), you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to?WinZip.exe?or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting?Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded?.zip?file(s).Extract the contents of the file(s) to your?<AUTOSAR>?folder.

Summary: In deployed standalone applications with a GUI, some graphics callback events are lost or are not as responsive as MATLAB
Description: In deployed MATLAB code that utilizes a GUI with callback functions, the assigned functions might miss some callback events because some GUI events are lost or are not responding.  This translates to slow GUI response or random response behavior.
Workaround: Use the waitfor MATLAB command to prevent the MATLAB code from exiting while the figure is open. This will allow the GUI figure to perform normally.For example, deploying the following code works:%code that generates or runs the GUIwaitfor(gcf);The waitfor can appear at the very end of your deployed application code.

Summary: If a handle class has the same name as its package, instantiating the class from within its class methods can cause an error.
Description: If a handle class has the same name as the package that contains the class definition, attempting to call the class constructor from a method causes the following error:  One or more output arguments not assigned during call to "_mcos_superclass_implicit_construct".  For example, create +foo/foo.m   classdef foo < handle     properties         prop     end     methods         function obj = foo(arg)             obj.prop = arg;         end     end     methods (Static)         function obj = createInstance(arg)             obj = foo.foo(arg);         end     end end  Calling  foo.foo.creatInstance('abc') causes the error.
Workaround: Do not use the same name for the package and the class.

Summary: SystemVue is unable to use my installation of MATLAB to execute MATLAB code.
Description: When MATLAB code is executed at the SystemVue command prompt with "Use MATLAB R2015b" or "Use MATLAB R2016a" selected, the following errors are displayed at the command prompt: Error: Failed to start Expression Engine evaluation.Error during serialization
Workaround: Installation instructionsRestart your computer and log in to your computer with an account that has administrator privileges. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Right click on the MATLAB icon and select "Run as Administrator" to launch a new instance of MATLAB.Locate your MATLAB installation directory by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, if corresponding file exists in your <MATLAB> folder or sub folders, make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <MATLAB> <.zip filename>At the MATLAB prompt, execute this code:cd(matlabroot);versionStr = version;if ~isempty(strfind(versionStr,'R2015b')) || ... ~isempty(strfind(versionStr,'R2016a')) patchName = sprintf('attachment_1310916_%s_%s_2016-06-22.zip', ...                           versionStr(end-3:end-1),computer('arch'))  unzip(patchName, matlabroot);endDelete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Slow open-loop neural network training using trainlm or trainbr
Description: If you train an open-loop time-series neural network using Levenberg-Marquardt trainlm  or Bayesian regularization trainbr , it can be much slower in R2015b compared with R2015a. The slowdown is proportional to the number of time-steps in the open loop, so networks with very short open loops are not slower. Static networks, closed-loop dynamic networks, and gradient training algorithms are not affected by this bug.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB>  in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: MATLAB? can crash when multiple, variable-size arrays with different upper bounds are passed to the same function
Description: In certain cases, MATLAB can crash during code generation when multiple, variable-size arrays with different upper bounds are passed to the same function. The crash file contains this message: SEGV: Assertion at lowermatrixtypesforbackend.cpp line 651 The following example reproduces this problem. This example requires the DSP System Toolbox? software. Write the function myfcn .  function y = myfcn(n)%#codegenassert(isa(n, 'double'));hDFT = dsp.FFT();s = zeros(1,n);y = subfcn(s,hDFT);function y = subfcn(s,hDFT)a = abs(step(hDFT,s(1:228)));b = abs(step(hDFT,s(1:240)));y = a(1) + b(1);  Generate code for myfcn . codegen myfcn
Workaround: Try to unite the variable-size arrays that have different upper bounds into one variable-size array that has an upper bound that is large enough for all of the arrays. In the previous example, use coder.varsize to define a a variable-size array t with an upper bound of 240. Instead of passing  s(1:228) and s(1:240) to abs, pass t to abs.function y = myfcn(n)%#codegenassert(isa(n, 'double'));hDFT = dsp.FFT();s = zeros(1,n);y = subfcn(s,hDFT);function y = subfcn(s,hDFT)coder.varsize('t', [1 240]);t = s(1:228);a = abs(step(hDFT,t));t = s(1:240);b = abs(step(hDFT,t));y = a(1) + b(1);

Summary: Simulation results may differ between normal mode and accelerated mode
Description: Simulation results may differ between normal mode and accelerated mode for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains two instances of a subsystem that has the Function packaging  parameter set to Reusable function . The two subsystem instances execute at different rates.
Workaround: When simulating the model in accelerated mode, set the Function packaging parameter to Nonreusable function or Inline.

Summary: Simulation error when logging simulation statistics for model with multiple physical networks
Description: If a model containing multiple physical networks has Simscape data logging enabled, with the Log simulation statistics  check box selected, simulation might fail with an error: Cannot add scope with id 'values' since a scope with that id already exists in the current scope. ? ?
Workaround: Disable logging simulation statistics as part of Simscape data logging, or reconfigure the model to avoid multiple physical networks.To disable statistics logging, either open the Simscape pane of model configuration parameters and clear the Log simulation statistics check box, or issue the following command:set_param(model_name,'SimscapeLogSimulationStatistics','off')where model_name is the name of your model.

Summary: ?S-function parameterized by reference to struct parameter may cause model build to fail
Description: A model may fail to build if it ?contains an S-Function with parameter usage as follows: The S-function is parameterized by a reference to a struct  field. The struct  field has an array type.  ?

Summary: Timeouts and inconsistent resolution when using Webcam snapshot with Windows 10
Description: When using the snapshot  function with webcam  in MATLAB on Windows 10, sometimes the frames returned are not consistent with the current resolution, and the call to snapshot  also times out for some cameras. For example: >> cam = webcam('Logitech HD Pro Webcam C920'); >> c.Resolution = '1920x1080';>> img = snapshot(c); >> size(img); % This would return 480x640x3, which is incorrect >> c.Resolution = '800x600'; >> img = snapshot(c); Error using matlab.webcam.internal.WebcamController/getCurrentFrame (line 99) Time out occurred while trying to get a frame from the webcam. Error in webcam/snapshot (line 227)            [image, timestamp] = obj.CamController.getCurrentFrame();

Summary: Internal assertion error due to specifying value from an enumeration class that derives from type other than Simulink.IntEnumType
Description: Simulink Coder might produce an assertion error during code generation for the following modeling pattern: A For Each subsystem contains a Constant block. The Constant value  parameter contains an enumerated type that derives from a type other than Simulink.IntEnumType . For example, a MATLAB enumeration definition that begins as follows:      classdef(Enumeration) Enum_int8 < int8 In the above example, this enumeration class derives from an?int8  type.  The assertion error contains the following text:? Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: b:\ matlab\ src\ cg_ir\ base_core\ factory\ constfactory.cppline 1083: aType->ordinalIsInRange(aOrdinal)?
Workaround: Use an enumeration class that derives from?Simulink.IntEnumType.

Summary: On a Linux system using NCurses version 6, MATLAB might crash on startup
Description: A major update to NCurses, version 6, has been released and is being incorporated into some Linux distributions. On Linux distributions where only NCurses 6 is installed, MATLAB might crash and fail to start.
Workaround: Install the NCurses 5 package for your Linux distribution.

Summary: Double-clicking on high precision numbers in the Variable Editor without editing them may cause a loss of precision
Description: In the Variable Editor, double-clicking on a cell that contains more than 15 digits after the decimal point causes content after the 15 digits to be lost if you click away or press the Enter  key.
Workaround: If you double-click in a cell without intending to edit the value, press the Esc key to revert back to the initial value.

Summary: Slow performance when changing to a folder located inside a Git repository
Description: Git? integration in MATLAB tracks the status of every file under the Git project root location. This includes the .git  folder, which maintains the revision history of the repository.  A performance degradation occurs when the file/folder structure under the root is very large.
Workaround: If you are not using source control features within MATLAB, you can disable source control integration.On the Home tab, in the Environment section, click Preferences.In the Preferences dialog box, in the MATLAB > General > Source Control pane, select None.

Summary: Embedded Coder generates C code that references an undefined variable when a model contains multiple instances of subsystems connected to Unit Delay blocks
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? Embedded Coder may generate C Code that is not compilable because it references an undefined variable when a model meets these conditions: The model contains a subsystem that has a Unit Delay block which directly connects to the subsystem's output port. There are multiple instances of the subsystem In the subsystem block parameters dialog box, Function packaging  is set to Reusable Function . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Structure reference . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Optimize global data access  parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results? or?Minimize global data access.
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Generating code for a model with a Gain block may result in a warning regarding the use of a ufix1 type
Description: When generating code for a model containing one or more Gain blocks, the following warning may display: Warning: The data type "ufix1" uses a word size that is not available on the intended target. Fixed-point signals using this data type will be put inside a larger word or multi words. When used, extra software will be generated to force this larger word or multi words to emulate a smaller word. This emulation is helpful when your prototype target and your final production target are not the same. If the smaller word size does NOT exist on the final production target, then consider increasing the word size to one that is supported. Code generation and simulation are correct and are unaffected by this warning. This warning appears when all  the following conditions are true. The Parameter data type  of the Gain block is set to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule . The Gain block has a gain value of 0. The input signal to the Gain block is of type Boolean .  Attached is a MATLAB script detectGainBlkIssue_1299464  to help detect instances of this bug. NOTE: This script requires a Fixed-Point Designer license. This script is meant only as a diagnostic tool. This script compiles the input model in the current working directory.  To use this script: Download files detectGainBlkIssue_1299464.m  and detectGainBlkIssue_1299464.p  into your current working directory, or into a directory on the MATLAB path. At the MATLAB command prompt, enter the following command with a valid Simulink model name:ret = detectGainBlkIssue_1299464(model)   This script looks at the input model and returns a vector of block handles for the Gain blocks which use a parameter runtime data type of ufix1 .
Workaround: Perform one of the following to work around this bug:Change the Parameter data type on the Gain block to an explicit type (for example,  uint8).Change the data type of the input signal to the Gain block to a type other than Boolean.Change the value of the Gain parameter on the Gain block to a value other than 0.

Summary: Parameter tuning in the presence of other errors can fail silently
Description: In certain cases, tuning parameters during simulation can fail to change the simulation result if either  of these conditions is met: A function referenced by a block is modified in a way which causes a parameter evaluation error. (R2016a only) A parameter on a block in a referenced model is modified while there are many other parameter evaluation errors. This only applies to Fast Restart simulations.  In either case, a subsequent update diagram reports success, but may not tune the parameter values of these blocks.
Workaround: To address this problem, use one of these approaches: After you have resolved the errors, call an additional set_param on the affected block.After you have resolved the errors, open the parameter dialog box of the affected block and press OK.Add a reference to a base workspace variable from the affected block.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during startup on Ubuntu 15.04 and newer, as well as distributions derived from those versions
Description: When using Ubuntu Linux distributions 15.04 and newer, as well as distributions derived from those versions, MATLAB can crash during startup. This crash occurs because these releases include a newer version of libstdc++.so.6 than the version shipped with MATLAB (version 6.0.17). When MATLAB loads version 6.0.17 first, the OS reaches an incompatibility that causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: You can force MATLAB to load the newer version of the library provided by the operating system, by following these instructions:Identify the location where MATLAB is installedNavigate to the sys/os/glnxa64 directory within this installation folderRename libstdc++.so.6 library to libstdc++.so.6.oldWe have done limited testing with version 20 of libstdc++.so.6.  If you experience problems with MATLAB when using this version, please contact MathWorks technical support.

Summary: False MISRA C2012 Rule 10.1 warnings might occur
Description: The Model Advisor might produce a false warning of a MISRA C2012 Rule 10.1 violation for the Check for bitwise operations on signed integers  check. These warnings might occur for the following modeling pattern: The model contains a Stateflow Chart or MATLAB Function block that uses unsigned operands in either the model or generated code. The operands are implemented in a smaller type than the target 'unsigned int '. For example, uint8  and uint16  type operands may cause the false warnings on a target that provides 8-bit, 16-bit and 32-bit built-in integers.  Here is an example of a Stateflow classic action language statement:  ?y = u1 & u2 ;  ?where u1  and u2  are declared as uint8 ? in any scope. ?Here is a MATLAB example: ?y = bitand(uint8(u1),uint8(u2));  ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Certain operations might be evaluated unexpectedly when used in a logical expression
Description: If you use certain operations like cast or array indexing as part of a logical expression, the operations might get incorrectly evaluated outside of the logical expression in the generated code. This issue affects Stateflow transitions, MATLAB functions in Stateflow, MATLAB Function blocks, and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink. For example,?a transition condition of: [u < arrayLen && int32(myArray[u]) < 5]  might generate the following code: tmp = myArray[u];?if (tmp < MAX_INT) {?? ?if (tmp > MIN_INT) {?? ? ? tmp_0 = tmp;?? ?} else {?? ? ? tmp_0 = MIN_INT;?? ?}?} else {??? ?tmp_0 = MAX_INT;?}?if (u < arrayLen && tmp_0 < 5) {?...??  Notice that the array is indexed in the first line, before checking whether or not the index is valid.? ?
Workaround: Break up the array indexing or cast operation into three transition segments. The first transition?contains the index check. The second consists of a call to an empty Simulink function. The third transition consists of the second part of the original logical expression. For example, the first transition could have:[u < arrayLen]?followed by a second transition with:?{emptyfcn();}followed by a third transition with:?[int32(myArray[u]) < 5]?

Summary: Setting the TableColSpecGroup.Span property incorrectly breaks a PDF report
Description: In PDF output type, setting the TableColSpecGroup.Span  property to less than the sum of its child column spec spans causes an error when the document is closed. Also, if the sum of? TableColSpecGroup.Span  property values of all the groups is less than the total number of columns in the table, an error may occur. For example: import mlreportgen.dom.*  % Create a table, one colum spec group and 3 column specstable = Table(magic(5)); group = TableColSpecGroup(); specs(3) = TableColSpec(); specs(2) = TableColSpec(); specs(1) = TableColSpec(); specs(1).Style = { Color('red') }; specs(2).Style = { Color('blue') }; specs(3).Style = { Color('red') }; group.ColSpecs = specs;   % Set Span to 2 when the right value should be 5group.Span = 2; table.ColSpecGroups = group;     rpt = Document('output','pdf'); append(rpt, table); close(rpt); rptview(rpt.OutputPath);    Causes this error FATAL: The column-number or number of cells in the row overflows the number of fo:table-columns specified for the table. (See position 1:2380) Error using rptgen.utils.FOPProxy.foToPDF
Workaround: Set the TableColSpecGroup.Span property to be greater than or equal to the number of column specs in the group. ?The sum of? TableColSpecGroup.Span property values in all the groups must match the number of columns in the table.

Summary: Call to a function-call subsystem can cause an overflow during code generation
Description: When a block inside a function-call subsystem requires absolute time, Simulink fails to provide a 64-bit clocktick counter when either of these conditions exist: Multiple function-call generators call the function-call subsystem through a Mux block. A wide asynchronous function-call generator calls the function-call subsystem.  The lack of a 64-bit clocktick counter can cause an overflow when code generation runs for a very long time.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.?

Summary: Computer may become unresponsive or restart when running a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model.
Description: When you are executing a real-time application using the Simulink Desktop Real-Time product on Microsoft Windows 8, Microsoft Windows 8.1, or Microsoft Windows 10, the computer sometimes becomes unresponsive or restarts unexpectedly. This problem is more likely to happen when execution runs for more than 5-7 minutes. The problem exists because the Simulink Desktop Real-Time kernel is incompatible with certain system hardware when the hardware is controlled by Microsoft Windows 8 and newer operating systems.
Workaround: This workaround is intended only for computers running Microsoft Windows 8, 8.1, or 10. Please do not apply the workaround to computers running Windows 7.As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files, named according to the release they support.Select and download the workaround file for your release.Locate the directory of the downloaded file. This directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Type matlabroot to locate your MATLAB installation. This directory is referred to as <MATLABROOT> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Navigate to <DOWNLOAD> and open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to <MATLABROOT>.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type sldrtkernel -uninstall to uninstall the Simulink Desktop Real-Time kernel.At the MATLAB command prompt, type sldrtkernel -setup to install the new Simulink Desktop Real-Time kernel.July 27, 2016: attachment_1273741_15a_win64_2016-07-27.zip: works on R2015a Win64 onlyJuly 27, 2016: attachment_1273741_15b_win64_2016-07-27.zip: works on R2015b Win64 onlyJuly 27, 2016: attachment_1273741_16a_win64_2016-07-27.zip: works on R2016a Win64 only

Summary: Build process for custom target incorrectly uses MinGW compiler rather than TMF-specified compiler on Windows?
Description: The build process for custom targets incorrectly uses MinGW instead of the compiler selected in the template makefile (TMF) under the following conditions: mex -setup  was used to set the default MEX compiler to MinGW. The model configuration uses a TMF-compliant custom target file (for example, not a toolchain-compliant target). The TMF selects a compiler other than MinGW.
Workaround: Either do not set the default MEX compiler to MinGW or install the software from the .zip file associated with this Bug Report.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change log: Feburary 26, 2016: attachment_1271231_15b_win64_2016-02-26.zip: initial version, works on R2015b and win64 only

Summary: Zooming on subplots with datetime values does not scale ticks and tick labels correctly
Description: If you plot a datetime array in a subplot, zoom in on a narrow range of dates or times, and zoom out, then the ticks and tick labels might not update correctly. Create a subplot with a datetime variable on the x-axis:     dtArray = datetime(2015,6,10)+days(1:10);    subplot(2,1,1); plot(dtArray, randn(size(dtArray)));    subplot(2,1,2); plot(dtArray, randn(size(dtArray)));  Zoom in to show about one hour on the x-axis. Zoom out.  The ticks and tick labels of the zoomed plot now overlap each other in the middle of the x-axis. Note: the same issue exists when you plot a datetime array along the y-axis in a subplot.
Workaround: Slightly resize the figure window or pan along the datetime axis to restore proper scaling of ticks and tick labels.

Summary: Playing audio using the sound function might crash MATLAB? on Windows?
Description: On some Windows? systems, playing audio using sound  might crash MATLAB?. For example, the following command crashes MATLAB: load handel;sound(y, Fs)  The same crash can be reproduced by executing the following commands: load handel;ap = audioplayer(y, Fs);play(ap)  or info = audiodevinfo

Summary: MATLAB? on Ubuntu? version 15.04 and higher fails without warning when reading data from MP3, MP4, and MOV files
Description: If you are using Ubuntu? version 15.04 or higher, MATLAB? may fail without warning while reading some audio and video files.  For example [y, Fs] = audioread('myaudiofile.mp3');  vidObj = VideoReader('myvideofile.mp4');  will cause MATLAB to fail. File formats include MP3, MP4 and MOV.
Workaround: To work around this issue replace the version of libstdc++.so 6 library shipped with MATLAB? with the native version on your system.To do this, first complete the following steps to ensure that the system's libstdc++.so.6 version is higher than the one shipped with MATLAB?.1. In a Linux terminal, navigate to matlabroot/sys/os/glnxa64 and type the following: ls -lThe version of the library shipping with MATLAB? should be  libstdc++.so.6.0.17.2. Navigate to /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu and type the following: ls -l libstdc++*If the resulting version is higher than 6.0.17, then continue with the following steps.1. Navigate to matlabroot/sys/os/glnxa64.2. Execute the following:unlink libstdc++.so.6ln -s /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libstdc++.so.6 libstdc++.so.63. Restart MATLAB?.

Summary: Generated code may contain arrays?with fixed upper bounds instead of dimension variants for exported subsystem functions
Description: The generated code for a subsystem may contain arrays with fixed upper bounds instead of dimension variants when you right-click the subsystem and select C/C++ Code>Export Functions .
Workaround: Convert the subsystem to a referenced model and then?generate code for the top model.?The code generator exports functions for the referenced model.

Summary: Code generation can result in unreachable code when configuration parameter AdvancedOptControl is set to -SLCI
Description: Code generation can result in unreachable code when configuration parameter AdvancedOptControl  is set to -SLCI  if the model uses an Abs block with an input signal of data type integer.
Workaround: Cast the input signal to a floating-point data type before the Abs block.

Summary: Unable to call a static method to define parameters in a matlab.unittest.TestCase class
Description: Within a TestCase  subclass, using a static method to define default values for a TestParameter , MethodSetupParameter , or ClassSetupParameter  results in an error. For example, to define a property, Param , with a TestParameter  attribute, the following class calls the ParamViaStaticMethod.getParam  static method. classdef ParamViaStaticMethod < matlab.unittest.TestCase    properties (TestParameter)        Param = ParamViaStaticMethod.getParam;    end     methods (Static)        function value = getParam            value = {'a'};        end    end    methods (Test)        function test1(~, Param)        end    endend When you run the test, it produces an error similar to: The class ParamViaStaticMethod has no property or method named 'getParam'.
Workaround: To define the parameter, use a local function instead of a static method.  The test in the following class runs without error.classdef ParamViaLocalFunction < matlab.unittest.TestCase    properties (TestParameter)        Param = getParam;    end    methods (Test)        function test1(~, Param)        end    endendfunction value = getParamvalue = {'a'};end

Summary: OS X Preview application might warn about missing fonts when viewing PostScript or Encapsulated PostScript files generated by MATLAB
Description: Viewing PostScript or Encapsulated PostScript files generated by MATLAB in the OS X Preview application can trigger warnings about missing fonts. The warnings can occur if the text in the MATLAB figure relies on Computer Modern fonts. For example, this code saves a PostScript file with text that relies on Computer Modern fonts. Using the Preview application to display the file triggers a warning that the CMMI10 and CMR10 fonts are not found and that Courier is being used instead. x = 1:10;  y = 1:10; f = figure; plot(x,y); title('Title \Delta','FontWeight','normal'); xlabel('X Label \beta');ylabel('Y Label \alpha'); print(f, '-dpsc2', 'matlab.eps');  ?
Workaround: Although not suitable for all purposes, you can generate the file using the OpenGL renderer, which embeds an image of the figure into the file rather than the vectorized instructions. Images do not require external fonts, but often do not scale well when resized. To generate the image with a specific size, use the figure's PaperPosition property. To generate the file using OpenGL, use one of these two options:Explicitly set the figure's renderer to OpenGL before calling print.set(f, 'Renderer', 'opengl');?Specify the -opengl option when calling print.print(f, '-depsc2', 'matlab.eps', '-opengl');?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table Dynamic block in a multi-instance reusable subsystem can generate incorrect code
Description: A Simulink model can generate incorrect code if it meets all of these conditions: The model has two or more identical atomic subsystems containing Lookup Table Dynamic blocks. The xdat  inputs of the lookup table blocks are driven by Constant blocks outside the subsystems. These Constant blocks have constant sample time. At least one of the Constant blocks has evenly spaced values. At least one other Constant block does have not evenly spaced values. The blocks directly feed Lookup Table Dynamic blocks inside two different reusable subsystems.  On building the model, the generated code for the block with not-evenly-spaced xdat points might incorrectly share the optimized algorithm for evenly spaced points. Hence, other instances may get incorrect output from their Lookup Table Dynamic blocks.
Workaround: To work around this problem, clear the Inline Parameters check box in Model Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Simulation and Code Generation and rebuild your model. Alternatively, change the sample time of the Constant block from Inf to -1.

Summary: Continuous audio signal generated using startBackground can have periodic gaps
Description: When using a DirectSound audio output device to generate continuous audio signals using a session  and startBackground , the quality of the output signal can be affected by periodic gaps. A typical workflow is to start the session in continuous background mode and queue additional data until the session is stopped. Gaps in the audio output signal can be observed for certain session rates and amounts of data queued. Refer to the following example: s = daq.createSession('directsound');addAudioOutputChannel(s, 'Audio3', '1', 'audio');s.Rate = 8000;  t = linspace(0, 0.5, 0.5*s.Rate+1);t(end) = [];y = sin(2*pi*800*t)';  lh = addlistener(s, 'DataRequired', ...  @(src, event) queueOutputData(src, y));elh = addlistener(s, 'ErrorOccurred', ...  @(src, event) disp(getReport(event.Error)));  s.IsContinuous = true;  queueOutputData(s, y);  startBackground(s);  ?

Summary: On Chinese, Japanese, and Korean computers, typing in the documentation search field has no effect
Description: When using the Help browser on computers using a Chinese, Japanese, or Korean language setting, typing search terms into the search field on documentation pages may have no effect.
Workaround: Use one of these approaches to work around this issue:Use the search field in the MATLAB desktop toolstrip- The top right corner of the MATLAB desktop contains a search field that you can use to search the documentation.Use documentation on the MathWorks web site- You can use a web browser to view documentation on the MathWorks web site at https://www.mathworks.com/help.Use a different HTML rendering system in MATLAB- You can change the HTML rendering system in MATLAB. On Windows, use: webutils.htmlrenderer('ie')or on Mac use: webutils.htmlrenderer('safari').Use PDF documentation- You can download MathWorks documentation in PDF format from the MathWorks web site and use your PDF viewer find functionality to search the documentation.

Summary: On Linux, annotation Select Font dialog box incorrectly displays foreign characters for some fonts
Description: On a Linux machine configured for English, the Select Font  dialog box incorrectly displays foreign characters for some fonts. Examples of fonts that show foreign characters when you select them include FreeSerif, FreeMono, and FreeSan.  To open the Select Font  dialog box, use one of these approaches: Select the annotation and from the context menu select Format > Font Style . Select text in an annotation and from the context menu select Font > Font Properties .
Workaround: On Linux machine, in the terminal, change the LANG environment variable and start MATLAB.setenv LANG en_US.utf-8 matlab

Summary: intlinprog can return a solution vector that is not integer feasible.
Description: Rarely, when intlinprog  returns a solution vector x  that has large-magnitude integer values, x  is not integer-valued to within the TolInteger  tolerance. You can check whether x  is integer feasible by entering  max(abs(x(intcon) - round(x(intcon))))

Summary: Inconsistent frame-based processing behavior for some Unit Delay blocks in the Additional Discrete library
Description: The following blocks in the Simulink/Additional Math & Discrete/Additional Discrete library produce inconsistent results when their input is a frame-based signal: Unit Delay Enabled External IC Unit Delay Enabled Resettable Unit Delay Enabled Resettable External IC Unit Delay External IC Unit Delay Resettable Unit Delay Resettable External IC  These blocks incorrectly overwrite the data in the first frame of the output with the initial condition. This bug affects only the first frame of the output. The results are correct for all subsequent output frames.
Workaround: 1. Discard the first frame from the output of these blocks.

Summary: Text generated by ylabel can overlap Y-axis tick labels of a plotmatrix graph.
Description: When you label a y-axis with the ylabel  function, it can overprint tick labels produced by plotmatrix . For example: %%% BEGIN CODE %%%M = repmat(100 * magic(4), 10, 1);plotmatrix(M);hx = xlabel('simple x-label');hy = ylabel('simple y-label');%%% END CODE %%%
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can manually adjust the position of the Y-label. To do this programmatically:%%% BEGIN CODE %%%ylpos = get(hy, 'position');set(hy, 'position', [ylpos(1) - 0.04, ylpos(2 : 3)]);%%% END CODE %%%To adjust the label interactively:Enter plot edit mode (Tools -> Edit plot)Select the Y-label by clicking itDrag the Y-label with the mouse to the left until it no longer overlaps the tick labels..

Summary: Callbacks behave unexpectedly when triggered through the .NET interface in Compile mode
Description: MATLAB's .NET interface support allows users to register a MATLAB function as a callback delegate. For instance, this can be used to invoke a MATLAB function when some event is initiated in .NET. This MATLAB function delegate can be called from .NET in the main thread or in a separate thread.  When invoked from MATLAB, both these use cases work successfully. However, in compiled mode (for example, with standalone applications created using MATLAB Compiler), the latter use case does not work; the callback delegate invoked in a separate thread fails and the application crashes. This happens especially if the callback MATLAB code is using global variables or MATLAB figure windows.
Workaround: Do not invoke the callback MATLAB code delegate in a separate thread.

Summary: Output sometimes appears in the wrong place when publishing a MATLAB file containing subfunctions
Description: When publishing a MATLAB file containing subfunctions, sometimes the output appears in the wrong place in the published document. For example, in the published document for this code, the "hi" should appear after the end of the main function, but instead appears after the subfunction. function fcnchalsubfunend%%function subfundisp hiend

Summary: Cannot expand axes to fill figure for subplot when the ActivePositionProperty is set to 'position'
Description: The Expand axes to fill figure  feature in the figure File -> Export Setup  dialog box does not expand subplots when the ActivePositionProperty of the subplot is set to the default value, 'position'. Reproduction steps: Execute this code in MATLAB   subplot(211);  plot(1:10);  subplot(212);  plot(1:10);  Select Export Setup...  from the figure's File  menu Click the check box for Expand axes to fill figure  to enable the feature Click the Apply to Figure  button  Notice that the two subplots do not expand to fill the figure.
Workaround: Set the ActivePostitionProperty of each of the subplot axes to 'outerposition' before using the Expand axes to fill figure feature: ax1 = subplot(211); plot(1:10); set(ax1,'ActivePositionProperty','outerposition'); ax2 = subplot(212); plot(1:10); set(ax2,'ActivePositionProperty','outerposition');Note that the property is modified after creating the plot because some commands, such as plotyy, will modify this property when they're run.

Summary: Saving Version 7.3 MAT-files silently fails when your system has insufficient disk space
Description: If you exceed your file system quota while saving a large Version 7.3 MAT-file, MATLAB fails to write the file, but does not return an error.  When you attempt to load the file, MATLAB returns an error, such as:??? Error using ==> load Can't read file U:\myfile.mat. During the save process, diagnostic information only appears in the terminal window used to start MATLAB.

Summary: plot(scalar,vector) may exhaust memory
Description: Compare the following usages of plot : plot(1:90000,1:90000);  % Works as expectedclose all;plot(1,1:90000);        % Exhausts memory
Workaround: Replace the second call to plot with:plot(ones(1,90000),1:90000);
